@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.gBXr3iB3qfBgmnCO]{Kuddle}

While it's possible to lightly contact a phased out eldamon in order to give it a pet or a hug, kuddle prefers the kind of prolonged contact that often requires either sharing phase in order to come into the physical world or melding into a creature in a sort of metaphysical hug. This philosophy makes them a bit unusual in the way they act, preferring to either find an eldamon trainer or to meld with a creature and remain a bit more active from within.

Seapia Filters

As a rare water eldamon, kuddle can help filter and purify water over time, especially while in their seapia or apamare form. While filtering water, they curl their little arms around the water, almost as if hugging it. Sometimes, when an area of water is particularly damaged by chemicals or other contaminants, multiple seapias must work together to form a powerful seapia filter, one day returning the water to its former pristine state.

Elemental Ability Tentacles of Water You can transform the motive force of your water powers into tendrils that throw off your foes' stance instead. If your water power would move a foe at least 10 feet, you can choose not to move the foe and instead make them off-guard until the beginning of your next turn. If your water power would move them at least 20 feet and you choose not to move them, they become off-guard until the end of your next turn, and if it would move them at least 40 feet and you choose not to move them, they become off-guard for 2 rounds.

Form Ability Grappling Arms Your many arms can easily grapple foes, even after using power attacks. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to Grapple, and your Athletics checks to Grapple don't apply the multiple attack penalty from powers with the attack trait (they still apply other multiple attack penalties, and they still increase your multiple attack penalty). If you're in your evolved form, the circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to Grapple increases to +2.

Evolution Ability Camouflage You can transform to any color necessary to blend in with your environment. You can Hide and Sneak even without cover or being concealed.

Lotusnek, Flower Snake Eldamon.name: 莲涅克，花蛇艾尔达蒙 Lotusnek, Flower Snake Eldamon
Lotusnek, Flower Snake Eldamon.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.sQbfAOo6lUGt5Ypb]{Lotusnek}

莲颈兽在木系艾达蒙之中相对常见，但这并不意味着其中许多个体会踏上冒险旅行。大多数莲颈兽会在葱郁之地安静冥想，参悟“三”的本质。

好事成三

人们常说好事成三。从技术上讲，他们也说厄运也会成三，但莲颈兽觉得没必要老想着消极的一面。寻找三件好事的组合，能帮助莲颈兽看到光明的一面。当然，莲颈兽会努力在周遭世界里找出一切“三”的组合，而且往往会过度。若你向莲颈兽指出一组对偶或二分，它们总会不知怎地伸展思路，提出第三个成员或选项。比如，你告诉莲颈兽某事物如同黑夜与白昼，它们反而可能提出由黑夜、白昼与黄昏构成的三元组。

莲颈兽、莲蛇与海德兰瑟

进化状态 帮助莲颈兽在其三位一体本性上达到最深层次的冥想。

元素能力 三之力 当你收获木元素能量时，你会以三倍之势将怒火倾泻于敌人。当你以造成伤害的力量进行收获时，你对其方格内有已被收获的plant的生物造成的该力量伤害获得+2状态加值。若你在力量DC上为专家，该加值提升为+4；若为大师则为+5；若为传奇则为+6。

形态能力 能量之花 你会缠绕着一朵充满木能量的花进行旅行（或者若你处于进化形态，则用你的三颗头达到相同效果）。这是一株播种在你自身空间中的plant，它会随你移动且不妨碍你的移动。当你首次进入战斗形态或进化形态时，这朵花会被充能，使你能够收获它。一旦被收获，这朵花在视觉上不会消失，但会失去能量，直到你下一次进入战斗或进化形态。

进化能力 三重时代 三颗头总比一颗好，因此将一个区域力量的效果分配给你的三颗头。当你在长度或半径至少为30 feet的锥形、线形或爆发区域中使用一个造成伤害的力量时，你可以改为制造三个更小的效果，每颗头各一个。若你如此做，你会制造三条线或三个锥形，其长度为原本的1/3（向下取整），或制造三个爆发，其半径为原本的1/3（向下取整）。即使某个生物处于多个锥形、线形或爆发的区域中，它也仍然只会承受该力量的效果一次。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.sQbfAOo6lUGt5Ypb]{Lotusnek}

Lotusneks are relatively common among wood eldamon, but that doesn't mean that many of them are traveling on adventures. Most lotusneks are quietly meditating on the nature of threes in verdant places.

Good Things

They say that good things come in threes. Technically, they also say bad luck comes in threes, but lotusneks see no need to dwell on the negative. Looking for triplets of good things. can help lotusnek look on the bright side. Of course, lotusneks try to find all the triples in the world around them, often to excess. Point out a pair or dichotomy to a lotusnek and they'll somehow stretch to produce a third member or option. For instance, if you tell a lotusnek that something is like night and day, they might posit a triumverate of night, day, and twilight instead.

Lotusnek, Lotuserpent, and Hydranther

Evolution Condition Helping lotusnek reach the deepest levels of meditation on their triune nature.

Elemental Ability Power of Three When you harvest wood elemental energy, you bring down your wrath on your foes threefold. When you harvest with a damaging power, you gain a +2 status bonus to the power's damage against creatures with a harvested plant in their square. This increases to +4 if you're an expert in power DC, +5 if you're a master in power DC, and +6 if you're legendary in power DC.

Form Ability Energy Flower You travel entwined around a flower filled with wood energy (or use your three heads for the same effect if you're in your evolved form). This is a sowed plant in your own space that moves with you and doesn't impair your movement. When you first enter battle form or evolved form, the flower is energized, allowing you to harvest it. Once harvested, the flower doesn't visually disappear, but it loses its energy until the next time you enter battle or evolved form.

Evolution Ability Triple Time Three heads are better than one, so split up an area power's effect among your three heads. When you use a damaging power in a cone, line, or burst with a length or radius of at least 30 feet, you can instead create three smaller effects, one from each head. If you do, you create three lines or cones with 1/3 the original length (rounded down) or three bursts with 1/3 the original radius (rounded down). Even if a creature is in the area of more than one of the cones, lines, or bursts, it still only takes the effects of the power once.

Peepkin, Pumpkin Eldamon.name: 皮普金，南瓜艾达蒙 Peepkin, Pumpkin Eldamon
Peepkin, Pumpkin Eldamon.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.NCSKLQmAjegiR1rS]{皮普金}

对皮普金而言，每一天都值得庆祝，这也是为什么它们喜欢布置装饰，并全力以赴，让周围的生物尽情享受节庆、派对以及其他重大节日。皮普金对凡人如何庆祝充满好奇，并且愿意花上数小时聆听当地根源。

葫芦邻居

皮普金经常栖居在农作物田野中，尤其是葫芦作物间；那里充盈着木元素能量，因此它们特别容易遇到葫芦莱西以及其他类似的植物生物。皮普金非常喜爱这类邻居，因为这能让它们有对象来欣赏自己的装饰，并与它们一同庆祝。唯一的例外，是那些过分严肃、认为装饰是在亵渎自然的植物生物。

皮普金、葫卫与南瓜骑士

非普通

ELDAMON

植物

木

元素 木

属性调整值 力量 +0，敏捷 +3，体质 +2，智力 -1，感知 +4，魅力 +1

艾达兽生命值 6

艾达兽技能 特技，威吓，潜行

艾达兽豁免 反射

感官 弱光视野

抗力 大地，植物，时代，水，木

弱点 强酸，空气，寒冷，火，毒素

速度 25尺

近战 1 爪击（灵巧，娴熟；仅能在战斗形态中使用）；伤害 1d4 挥砍

近战 1 刀刃臂（娴熟，触及 10尺，多用P；仅能在进化形态中使用）；伤害 1d8 挥砍

进化状态 帮助皮普金以真正精彩的装饰进行庆祝。

元素能力 丰收庆典 每当你进行收获时，你都会在木之能量流经体内时庆祝。在使用一个威能并选择进行收获后，若你至少收获了1个植物，你在1轮内获得对察觉检定、技能检定与豁免的+1状态加值。若你的威能DC为传奇，则你改为获得对察觉检定、技能检定与豁免的+2状态加值。

形态能力 不给糖就捣蛋 你对用于“挫志”的威吓检定获得+1环境加值。当你在一个其中有敌人的方格内收获植物时，该敌人不再对你的“挫志”暂时免疫。

进化能力 木剑与盾牌 你的爪击变形为木剑与盾牌的形状。这会将你的爪击徒手攻击改为刀刃臂徒手攻击，造成1d8挥砍伤害，失去灵巧特性，并获得多用P特性；上述数据已反映此变化。此外，你可以用你的盾臂来“举起护盾”，其效果如同一面硬度10、60 HP且BT为30的木制盾牌。你获得“护盾格挡”反应动作（），但只能用盾臂来使用。当你刷新时，你的盾臂会恢复其所有生命值，即使它曾被摧毁。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.NCSKLQmAjegiR1rS]{Peepkin}

Every day's worthy of celebration to a peepkin, and that's why they love setting up decorations and going all out to make the most of festivals, parties, and other big holidays for the creatures around them. Peepkins are curious about how mortals celebrate and will listen for hours about local traditions.

Gourd Neighbors

Peepkins often dwell in fields of crops, especially gourds, rife with wood elemental energy, which makes them especially likely to run into gourd leshies and other similar plant creatures. Peepkins absolutely adore having these sorts of neighbors, as it gives them someone to admire their decorations and celebrate alongside them. The only exception are overserious plant creatures who consider decorations to defile nature.

Peepkin, Gourdian, and Pumpkinight

UNCOMMON

ELDAMON

PLANT

WOOD

Element Wood

Attribute Modifiers Str +0, Dex +3, Con +2, Int -1, Wis +4, Cha +1

Eldamon Language Muan

Eldamon Hit Points 6

Eldamon Skills Acrobatics, Intimidation, Stealth

Eldamon Saving Throw Reflex

Senses low-light vision

Resistance earth, plant, time, water, wood

Weaknesses acid, air, cold, fire, poison

Speed 25 feet

Melee 1 claw (agile, finesse; only usable in battle form); Damage 1d4 slashing

Melee 1 blade arm (finesse, reach 10 feet, versatile P; only usable in evolved form); Damage 1d8 slashing

Evolution Condition Helping peepkin celebrate with truly wonderful decorations.

Elemental Ability Harvest Celebration Whenever you harvest, you celebrate as the wood energy courses through you. After using a power and choosing to harvest, if you harvested at least one plant, you gain a +1 status bonus to Perception checks, skill checks, and saving throws for 1 round. If you're legendary in power DCs, you instead gain a +2 status bonus to Perception checks, skill checks, and saving throws.

Form Ability Trick or Treat You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Intimidation checks to Demoralize. When you harvest plants in a square with a foe in it, that foe is no longer temporarily immune to your Demoralize.

Evolution Ability Wooden Sword and Shield Your claws transform into the shape of a wooden sword and shield. This changes your claw unarmed attack into a blade arm unarmed attack that deals 1d8 slashing damage, loses the agile trait, and gains the versatile P trait; this is already reflected in the statistics above. Additionally, you can use your shield arm to Raise a Shield, with the effects of a wooden shield with 10 Hardness, 60 HP and a BT of 30. You gain the Shield Block reaction, which you can only use with your shield arm. When you refresh, your shield arm recovers all its Hit Points, even if it was destroyed.

Squishberry, Fruit Snail Eldamon.name: 软浆莓，果蜗灵使盟友 Squishberry, Fruit Snail Eldamon
Squishberry, Fruit Snail Eldamon.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.nkQlg6hofiA0akeX]{软浆莓}

软浆莓热爱在花园里照料植物，它们的花园会变成充满自然奇观的乐园，拥有美丽的布置与稀有的植物。同样的培育本能也会用在照顾一群凡人时，不过软浆莓在那方面的技能要差得多，因此它们有时会提供对花园有效、却不一定适用于人的帮助。

照料自己的花园

对软浆莓而言，培育自己的花园是世界上最重要的任务——既能冥想，又极其私密。对软浆莓来说，打理花园是一种隐喻，用来整理它们自己的思绪与生活，专注于那些它们能立刻掌控的事，而不是被构成历史的事件洪流所裹挟。通过停止为无法控制之事担忧，软浆莓便能创造一座壮丽的花园，并在日复一日中成长一点点。

软浆莓、草莓螺旋兽与螺旋希昂

非普通

艾达兽

植物

木

元素 木

体型 软浆莓的进化形态草莓螺旋兽为超大型，而非大型。

属性调整值 Str +2, Dex +1, Con +3, Int -1, Wis +4, Cha +0

艾达兽语言 Muan

艾达兽生命值 6

艾达兽技能 杂技, 运动, 生存

艾达兽豁免 强韧

感官 弱光视野

抗力 大地, 植物, 时代, 水, 木

弱点 酸, 空气, 冷, 火, 毒

速度 25英尺

近战 1 身体（仅在进化时触及20英尺）；伤害 1d6 钝击（仅在进化时为1d8 钝击）

进化状态 帮助软浆莓打理一座拥有真正多样化植物配置的花园，或拥有几株尤其稀有的植物。

元素能力 园艺能手 当你播种植物时，你会播种更多。每当你用一个力量进行播种时，在你已经播种的区域相邻的一个方格中再播种一次。若你在力量DC上为专家，则在额外2个相邻方格中播种；若为大师，则在额外3个相邻方格中播种；若为传奇，则在额外4个相邻方格中播种。

形态能力 缩进草莓中 以单动作，你可以缩进你的壳中。若你如此做，你获得AC+2环境加值并陷入定身。这些效果持续到你下个回合结束，不过你可以用单动作提前结束该效果。

进化能力 巨量生长 你长得比大多数进化形态更大，而你那巨大身体的触及也比正常情况更远。你的体型为超大型，而非大型，并且你的身体拥有20英尺触及；该触及已反映在以上数据中。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.nkQlg6hofiA0akeX]{Squishberry}

Squishberries love taking caring of plants in their gardens, which become wonderlands of natural marvels with beautiful arrangements and rare plants. These same nurturing instincts apply when taking care of a group of mortals, though squishberries have a lot less skill in that arena, so they sometimes provide assistance that would work for a garden but not for a person.

Tending One's Garden

To squishberry, cultivating one's own garden is the most important task in the world, equal parts meditative and intensely personal. Tending a garden for a squishberry is a metaphor for getting their own thoughts and house in order, focusing on what they can immediately control rather than being swept away in the flow of events that make up history. By ceasing to worry over that which they can't control, squishberry is able to create a magnificent garden and grow a bit more each and every day.

Squishberry, Fragarios, and Helixion

UNCOMMON

ELDAMON

PLANT

WOOD

Weaknesses acid, air, cold, fire, poison

Speed 25 feet

Melee 1 body (reach 20 feet when evolved only); Damage 1d6 bludgeoning (1d8 bludgeoning when evolved only)

Evolution Condition Helping squishberry tend a garden with a truly diverse arrangement of plants, or a few especially rare plants.

Elemental Ability Green Thumb When you sow plants, you sow more. Any time you sow with a power, sow in a square adjacent to the area where you already sowed. If you're an expert in power DC, you sow in two additional adjacent squares, if you're a master in power DC, you sow in three additional adjacent squares, and if you're legendary in power DC, you sow in four additional adjacent squares.

Form Ability Retreat Into Strawberry As a single action, you can Retreat Into your Shell. If you do, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and are immobilized. These effects last until the end of your next turn, though you can end the effect early with a single action.

Evolution Ability Massive Growth You grow larger than most evolved forms, and the reach of your massive body is even greater than normal. You are Huge, instead of Large, and your body has a 20 foot reach; the reach has been reflected in the above statistics.

Arbush, Tree Eldamon.name: 阿布什，树木艾达蒙 Arbush, Tree Eldamon
Arbush, Tree Eldamon.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.xFXOBOQJPuOsRRo8]{树灌妖}

树灌妖是带着狡黠笑容的诡计型艾达兽，以树木的形态存在。取决于个人对玩笑与幽默的看法，树灌妖相当罕见这一事实，要么是货真价实的遗憾，要么是谢天谢地的恩赐。由于树灌妖本就很难被找到，而且它们通常只喜欢与那些共享其独特幽默感的人一起旅行，因此，拥有一只树灌妖伙伴的艾达兽训练师或其他凡人朋友确实极为罕见。

树灌妖的伏击

在讲笑话这件事上，时机很重要。偶尔，一个大家都看得出来要发生的笑话，仍然能漂亮收尾、效果拔群。更常见的是，当笑话从略微出乎预料之处出现时会更有效——回头想想合情合理，但事前并不可预测。树灌妖正是以这种幽默伏击而臭名昭著。

树灌妖、树战士与树堡垒

罕见

艾达兽

植物

木

元素 木

属性调整值 力量+1，敏捷+2，体质+3，智力+0，感知+4，魅力-1

艾达兽语言 穆安语

艾达兽生命值 6

艾达兽技能 运动、隐秘、生存

艾达兽豁免 强韧

感官 弱光视野

抗力 大地、植物、时代、水、木

弱点 强酸、风、寒冷、火、毒素

速度 25尺

近战 1 树枝（灵活、娴熟、仅在进化后触及10尺）；伤害 1d4 钝击（仅在进化后为1d6 钝击）

进化状态 帮助树灌妖成功实现一个完美的笑话，或一系列极佳的笑话。

元素能力 野性生长 当你播种植物时，你周围会长出更多植物。你每次以一个力量进行播种时，在你占据的一个方格或与你相邻的一个方格中播种。若你在力量DC上为专家，你在两个此类方格中播种；若你在力量DC上为大师，你在三个此类方格中播种；若你在力量DC上为传奇，你在四个此类方格中播种。

形态能力 植行者 你无视由植物与真菌造成的任何困难或更高等级的困难地形，例如灌木、藤蔓与林下植被。即使被魔法操控的植物与真菌也不会阻碍你的前进。此外，你可以在由植物造成的困难地形区域中进行躲藏与潜行，包括你播种出来的植物，即使它们未提供隐蔽或掩蔽亦然。

进化能力 苔藓掩护 当你被植物包围时，你身上的苔藓会为你提供保护。每当你处于由植物造成的困难地形中时（包括你播种出来的植物），你的AC与反射豁免获得+1环境加值。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-ancestries.Item.xFXOBOQJPuOsRRo8]{Arbush}

Arbushes are grinning trickster eldamon in the form of trees. Depending on personal opinions when it comes to jokes and humor, the fact that arbushes are quite rare is either a real shame or a saving grace. Between how hard it is to find an arbush in the first place and the fact that arbushes usually like to travel only with those who share their particular sense of humor, an eldamon trainer or other mortal friend with an arbush companion is rare indeed.

Arbush Ambushes

Timing is important when it comes to jokes. Every once in a while, a joke that everyone can see coming can still stick a powerful landing. More often, a joke is more effective when it comes from just outside of expectations, enough that it makes sense in retrospect but wasn't predictable in advance. Arbushes are notorious for these humorous ambushes.

Arbush, Arborrior, and Arbastion

RARE

ELDAMON

PLANT

WOOD

Element Wood

Attribute Modifiers Str +1, Dex +2, Con +3, Int +0, Wis +4, Cha - 1

Eldamon Language Muan

Eldamon Hit Points 6

Eldamon Skills Athletics, Stealth, Survival

Eldamon Saving Throw Fortitude

Senses low-light vision

Resistance earth, plant, time, water, wood

Weaknesses acid, air, cold, fire, poison

Speed 25 feet

Melee 1 branch (agile, finesse, reach 10 feet when evolved only); Damage 1d4 bludgeoning (1d6 bludgeoning when evolved only)

Evolution Condition Helping arbush pull off the perfect joke, or a series of excellent jokes.

Elemental Ability Wild Growth When you sow plants, more plants grow around you. Any time you sow with a power, sow in a square you occupy or a square adjacent to you. If you're an expert in power DC, you sow in two such squares, if you're a master in power DC, you sow in three such squares, and if you're legendary in power DC, you sow in four such squares.

Form Ability Plantwalker You ignore any difficult or greater difficult terrain caused by plants and fungi, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth. Even plants and fungi manipulated by magic don't impede your progress. Additionally, you can Hide and Sneak in areas of difficult terrain caused by plants, including plants you sowed, even if they don't provide concealment or cover.

Evolution Ability Moss Cover When you're surrounded by plants, the moss on your body provides you protection. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your AC and Reflex saves whenever you are in difficult terrain caused by plants, including plants you sowed.

Table 5: Eldamon Age and Background.name: 表 5：艾达蒙的年代与背景 Table 5: Eldamon Age and Background
Table 5: Eldamon Age and Background.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.xQUNJgsEb0ef3vU3]{表5：艾达蒙年代与背景}

d% 掷骰年代与背景
01–10新生 这只艾达蒙才刚出生不久。他们无法说或理解任何语言，对周围的世界也知之甚少。
11–30幼年 这只艾达蒙相对年轻，除了自身元素之外几乎没有经验。他们只会说并理解其条目中列出的语言。
31–80典型 这只艾达蒙在某个区域生活得足够久，能够理解凡人，并在其所列语言之外学会当地通行的语言（通常是通用语）。
81–90元素使者 这只艾达蒙曾与另一种元素的艾达蒙一同生活，并且与他们相处融洽。在表上掷骰
91–100远古沉眠者 这只艾达蒙自远古时代以来一直未曾活跃，他们对世界的理解基于过时的信息。除其所列语言外，他们还会说并理解一种远古的、已死的或深奥的语言。如果你的世界没有任何此类语言，则使用一种由特别长寿的血统所使用的语言，例如龙语。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.xQUNJgsEb0ef3vU3]{Table 5: Eldamon Age and Background}

d% RollAge and Background
01–10Newborn The eldamon has only recently been born. They can’t speak or understand any language and know little of the world around them.
11–30Young The eldamon is relatively young with little experience beyond their own element. They speak and understand only the language listed in their entry.
31–80Typical The eldamon has lived in an area long enough to understand mortals and pick up the prevailing language (usually Common) in addition to their listed language.
81–90Elemental Envoy The eldamon has lived among eldamon of another element and gets along with them well. Roll on Table
91–100Ancient Slumberer The eldamon hasn’t been active since ancient times, and their understanding of the world is based on outdated information. They speak and understand one ancient, dead, or esoteric language, in addition to their listed language. If your world doesn’t have any such languages, use a language spoken by particularly long-lived ancestries, such as Draconic.

Table 6: Elemental Ability Variation.name: 表 6：元素能力变体 Table 6: Elemental Ability Variation
Table 6: Elemental Ability Variation.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.SikxK9DSdvWvHoY3]{掷骰表 6：元素能力变体}

d% 掷骰能力变体
01–50无变体
51–55替代起始力量 该eldamon以一个与其同类与等级的eldamon正常情况下不同的力量开始。
56–60替代力量组 该eldamon以其元素的另一只随机eldamon的力量组开始。
61–65替代豁免 该eldamon拥有另一种随机eldamon豁免。
66–70替代技能 该eldamon将其一个eldamon技能替换为以下列表中的另一个随机技能：特技、运动、威吓、表演、潜行、求生或盗窃。eldamon绝不会替换其某个能力所需要的技能。
71–75替代元素能力 该eldamon拥有其元素的另一只随机eldamon通常具有的元素能力。这不会改变其形态或进化能力。
76–80辉光 该eldamon如同火把一般以元素能量发光（或者若其为通常会发光的火或光eldamon，则不发光）。
81–85异常亲和 使用表3选择两种该eldamon通常既不脆弱也不抗性的随机元素。该eldamon对其中一种元素相关的特性具有抗性，并对另一种元素相关的特性具有脆弱。这不能使该eldamon对物理伤害具有抗性或脆弱。
86–90异常攻击 该eldamon的一种徒手攻击若通常造成钝击、穿刺或挥砍伤害，则改为在这三种选项中造成另一种伤害类型。若该eldamon没有任何造成这些伤害类型的徒手攻击，则在掷骰表 6上重骰。
90–95强韧或灵巧 该eldamon交换其敏捷与体质调整值，基础与成长部分皆如此。
96–100强壮、聪慧或迷人 该eldamon在其力量、智力与魅力调整值中任意交换两项，基础与成长部分皆如此。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.SikxK9DSdvWvHoY3]{Table 6: Elemental Ability Variation}

d% RollAbility Variation
01–50No Variation
51–55Alternate Starting Power The eldamon starts with one power that’s different than normal for an eldamon of their kind and level.
56–60Alternate Power Set The eldamon starts with the power set from another random eldamon of their element.
61–65Alternate Saving Throw The eldamon has a different random eldamon saving throw.
66–70Alternate Skill The eldamon replaces one of its eldamon skills with a different random skill from this list: Acrobatics, Athletics, Intimidation, Performance, Stealth, Survival, or Thievery. An eldamon never replaces a skill they need for one of their abilities.
71–75Alternate Elemental Ability The eldamon has the elemental ability normally associated with another random eldamon of their element. This doesn’t change their form or evolution abilities.
76–80Glowing The eldamon glows with elemental energy like a torch (or doesn’t glow if they are a fire or light eldamon that normally glows).
81–85Unusual Affinities Choose two random elements to which the eldamon isn’t normally weak or resistant using Table 3. The eldamon is resistant to the traits associated with one of those elements and weak to the traits associated with the other element. This can’t make the eldamon resistant or weak to physical damage.
86–90Unusual Attacks One of the eldamon’s unarmed attacks that would normally deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage deals a different damage type from among those three options. If the eldamon has no unarmed attacks that deal any of these damage types, reroll on Table 6.
90–95Tough or Nimble The eldamon switches their Dexterity and Constitution modifiers, both for the base and for the progression.
96–100Strong, Smart, or Charming The eldamon switches any two of their Strength, Intelligence, and Charisma modifiers, both for the base and for the progression.

Table 7: Eldamon Appearance Feature.name: 表 7：艾尔达蒙外貌特性 Table 7: Eldamon Appearance Feature
Table 7: Eldamon Appearance Feature.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.RYpgym6CzaB4aLmg]{表7：Eldamon外观特性}

d% 掷骰外观特性
01–50标准外观 该eldamon看起来或多或少与同类的其他个体相似。
51–52更大 该eldamon明显比同类的其他个体更大，但仍属于相同的体型类别。
53–54更小 该eldamon明显比同类的其他个体更小，但仍属于相同的体型类别。
55–56不同的眼睛颜色 该eldamon的眼睛颜色与同类的其他eldamon不同。
57–58异色瞳 该eldamon的两只眼睛颜色不同（或者如果其种类通常具有不同颜色的眼睛，则两只眼睛为相同颜色）。
59–60不同的身体或毛发颜色 该eldamon的身体、毛发，或两者的颜色与同类的其他eldamon不同。
61–62半边半边的体色 该eldamon的身体、毛发，或两者仅在一侧（左侧或右侧）的颜色与同类的其他eldamon不同。
63–64鲜艳配色 该eldamon的配色比同类的其他eldamon更鲜艳。
65–66柔和配色 该eldamon的配色比同类的其他eldamon更柔和、更精致。
67–68灰度 该eldamon的配色完全为灰度；或者如果其种类通常全为灰度，则该eldamon包含颜色。
69–70变换的颜色 该eldamon的颜色会随时代推移而变换与改变。
71–72闪耀 该eldamon在光下闪闪发亮，看起来格外有光泽。
73–74近亲 该eldamon看起来像与同类相关但不同的动物或物体，例如用狐狸替代狼，或用银盘替代手镜。
75–76元素宝石 该eldamon的身体内有凝结的元素能量宝石；或者如果同类通常拥有元素宝石，则其体内的元素宝石具有不同的颜色或形状。
77–78元素纹路 该eldamon的身体上有循环元素能量的纹路；或者如果同类通常拥有元素纹路，则其体内的元素纹路具有不同的颜色或图案。
79–80元素灵光 该eldamon散发可见的元素能量灵光；或者如果同类通常拥有元素灵光，则其周身的元素灵光具有不同的颜色或形状。
81–82角与棘刺 该eldamon身上有小角、棘刺，或两者兼有，但不够大，不会造成伤害。
83–84凶悍 该eldamon的宠物形态看起来更像其战斗形态的缩小版本，而不是更可爱的形态。
85–86可爱 该eldamon的战斗形态与进化后的形态看起来更像其宠物形态的放大版本，并进行任何必要的调整以赋予其形态与进化能力。
87–88变化的形态 该eldamon的形态会随时代推移而轻微变化并变形。
89–90半透明的身体 该eldamon的身体允许光穿透，使其呈现半透明外观。
91–92缕雾般的身体 该eldamon的身体底部延伸并渐散为缕缕鬼火。
93–94讲究 该eldamon的形态看起来整洁、一丝不苟且保养得很好。
95–96狂野 该eldamon的形态看起来蓬乱而不受拘束。
97–98纯善 该eldamon的形态看起来圣洁而正直。
99–100凶邪 该eldamon的形态看起来邪魔般且凶恶。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.RYpgym6CzaB4aLmg]{Table 7: Eldamon Appearance Feature}

d% RollAppearance Feature
01–50Standard Appearance The eldamon looks more or less like others of their kind.
51–52Bigger The eldamon is noticeably bigger than others of their kind, though still the same size category.
53–54Smaller The eldamon is noticeably smaller than others of their kind, though still the same size category.
55–56Different Eye Color The eldamon’s eyes are a different color than other eldamon of their kind.
57–58Heterochromia The eldamon’s eyes are different colors (or the same color if eldamon of their kind normally have different colors).
59–60Different Body or Hair Color The eldamon’s body, hair, or both are a different color than other eldamon of their kind.
61–62Half and Half Body Color The eldamon’s body, hair, or both are a different color than other eldamon of their kind on just one side, either their left side or right side.
63–64Vibrant Coloration The eldamon’s coloration is more vibrant than other eldamon of their kind.
65–66Soft Coloration The eldamon’s coloration is softer and more delicate than other eldamon of their kind.
67–68Grayscale The eldamon’s coloration is all in grayscale, or if the eldamon’s kind normally are all in grayscale, the eldamon includes colors.
69–70Shifting Colors The eldamon’s colors shifting and change over time.
71–72Sparkly The eldamon sparkles in the light and looks especially shiny.
73–74Cousin The eldamon looks like a related but different animal or object than others of their kind, such as a fox instead of a wolf or a silver dish instead of a hand mirror.
75–76Elemental Gems Either the eldamon’s body has gems of congealed elemental energy in their body, or the elemental gems in their body are a different color or shape if others of their kind normally have elemental gems.
77–78Elemental Lines Either the eldamon’s body has lines that circulate elemental energy across their body, or the elemental lines in their body are a different color or pattern if others of their kind normally have elemental lines.
79–80Elemental Aura Either the eldamon exudes a visible aura of elemental energy, or the elemental aura around them is a different color or shape if others of their kind normally have an elemental aura.
81–82Horns and Spines The eldamon has small horns, spines, or both on their body, though not large enough to cause damage.
83–84Fearsome The eldamon’s pet form looks more like a smaller version of their battle form, rather than a cuter form.
85–86Cute The eldamon’s battle and evolved form look more like bigger version of the eldamon’s pet form, with any adjustments needed to grant them their form and evolution abilities.
87–88Shifting Form The eldamon’s form slightly shifts and transforms over time.
89–90Translucent Body The eldamon’s body allows light to pass through, giving it a translucent appearance.
91–92Wispy Body The eldamon’s body trails off into wisps at the bottom.
93–94Fastidious The eldamon’s form looks tidy and meticulously well kept.
95–96Wild The eldamon’s form looks disheveled and untamed.
97–98Wholesome The eldamon’s form looks saintly and wholesome.
99–100Baleful The eldamon’s form looks diabolical and baleful.

Table 9a: Check for Eldamon Secret.name: 表 9a：艾达蒙秘闻检定 Table 9a: Check for Eldamon Secret
Table 9a: Check for Eldamon Secret.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.SW2Ia7YE3it4dJIS]{掷骰表 9a：检定是否拥有艾达盟秘密}

d% 掷骰秘密
01–90该艾达盟没有特殊秘密。
91–99该艾达盟有一个特殊秘密。在掷骰表 9b 上掷骰以查看是什么。
100该艾达盟有两个秘密。在掷骰表 9b 上掷骰两次以确定它们。

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.SW2Ia7YE3it4dJIS]{Table 9a: Check for Eldamon Secret}

d% RollSecret
01–90The eldamon doesn’t have a special secret.
91–99The eldamon has a special secret. Roll on Table 9b to see what it is.
100The eldamon has two secrets. Roll twice on Table 9b to determine them.

Table 9b: Eldamon Secrets.name: 表9b：艾达蒙的秘密 Table 9b: Eldamon Secrets
Table 9b: Eldamon Secrets.text:
@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.ts2mpKVcbuDN6lmc]{掷骰表 9b：Eldamon 的秘密}

d% 掷骰秘密
01–03这只 eldamon 曾与另一位英雄或冒险者是朋友，无论对方是否出名。
04–06这只 eldamon 曾与一名反派是朋友，无论对方是否出名。
07–09这只 eldamon 曾融入过一件强力的神器或其他重要的魔法物品。
10–12这只 eldamon 曾融入过一个与其元素相符、极其危险的生物。
13–15这只 eldamon 曾融入过一场元素灾厄，例如海啸或火山喷发。
16–18这只 eldamon 曾向自己最后一位朋友许下承诺，要完成最后一项任务，而它至今仍未完成。
19–21这只 eldamon 发誓要守护久远以前那位老友的后裔。也许它需要帮助来追踪那些后裔，或者它的新朋友正是其中之一。
22–24这只 eldamon 正在寻找一件对它而言极其重要的失落物品。
25–27这只 eldamon 正在寻找一位老友，然而以它对时代的感知，那位朋友如今可能早已死去。
28–30这只 eldamon 是或曾是某个强大封印的元素支柱之一。封印之中封着或阻拦着什么？它与新朋友一同旅行，会帮助加固封印，还是会冒着将其打破的风险？
31–33这只 eldamon 曾知道某处失落宝藏或遗迹的位置。以它理解凡世的方式，它还能找到那里吗？它会带新朋友前往吗？
34–36这只 eldamon 因为曾做过某事或拥有某种才能，而在其他 eldamon 之间颇有名气。
37–39这只 eldamon 曾被某个社群奉为神祇或守护者。
40–42这只 eldamon 曾属于另一种元素的 eldamon，但不知为何，随着时间推移，它转变成了如今的元素与形态。
43–45这只 eldamon 失去了关于其一生中某个关键事件的全部记忆。
46–48不知为何，这只 eldamon 似乎总会吸引并受一种与其自身不同的元素所关注与影响。
49–51这只 eldamon 曾生活在一片遥远的土地上
52–54这只 eldamon 对凡世的一种特定零食或其他“罪恶快感”情有独钟，并且很想让一位 eldamon 训练师将它相位进去，好让它尽情享用。
55–57在无法刻意触发、且极其罕见的场合与环境下，这只 eldamon 会开启一个通往相关位面的微型临时传送门。
58–60这只 eldamon 知晓某位重要人物最深的秘密；然而鉴于它与世界交互的方式与它的记忆，它很可能完全不知道这条信息很重要。
61–63当这只 eldamon 遭遇到它所弱势的某种元素时，它会无法控制地打喷嚏，几乎像是过敏了一样。
64–66这只 eldamon 曾是 eldamon 之外的某种生物，因一种极为罕见的环境而重生或转化成 eldamon。
67–69这只 eldamon 暗中是一名间谍，为另一位最初的凡人朋友采集情报。这名间谍可能会更深切地在乎它新的凡人朋友而拒绝汇报；而即便它没有拒绝，由于 eldamon 的思维模式与记忆，那位最初的凡人朋友也并不指望自己的间谍能带来多少成果。
70–72这只 eldamon 被一个邪恶组织盯上，对方试图以未知原因捕获它。
73–75这只 eldamon 痴迷于某种特定形式的冷门学识，并希望尽可能随时满足自己的痴迷。
76–78这只 eldamon 在凡世自称拥有某种世俗头衔，却毫无实证，例如它宣称自己是某个遥远小镇的镇长，而那小镇的名字无人知晓。
79–81这只 eldamon 经常感到疲倦；当它进入休息状态时，通常会不小心融入附近的某个物体，直到它醒来。根据 eldamon 与该物体的不同，这可能是福也是祸。
82–84这只 eldamon 囤积与其元素相关的标记，并把它们藏在其凡人朋友的随身物品之中。
85–87这只 eldamon 源自另一个世界或现实的元素能量——并非与其他 eldamon 及其凡人朋友同一个世界。
88–90这只 eldamon 曾在暗中守护它的凡人朋友，当对方年幼之时。
91–93这只 eldamon 正试图赢得另一只 eldamon 的芳心，它可能会把这个秘密告诉自己的凡人朋友以寻求帮助。
94–96这只 eldamon 使用一个绰号，并将自己的真名保密。
97–100这只 eldamon 有一个秘密的性格怪癖（在表 8 上再掷骰一次），并选择将其隐藏。你在表上的原始掷骰

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.roll-tables.RollTable.ts2mpKVcbuDN6lmc]{Table 9b: Eldamon Secrets}

d% RollSecret
01–03The eldamon was once friends with another hero or adventurer, whether famous or not.
04–06The eldamon was once friends with a villain, whether famous or not.
07–09The eldamon once melded into a powerful artifact or other major magic item.
10–12The eldamon once melded into an incredibly dangerous creature that matches their element.
13–15The eldamon once melded into an elemental calamity, such as a tsunami or volcanic eruption.
16–18The eldamon made a promise to their last friend to perform one last task, and they still have yet to complete it.
19–21The eldamon has sworn to watch over the descendants of their old friend from long ago. Perhaps they need help keeping track of those descendants, or maybe their new friend is one of them.
22–24The eldamon is searching for a lost item of great importance to them.
25–27The eldamon is searching for an old friend, though with their sense of time, the friend might be long dead by now.
28–30The eldamon is or was one of the elemental pillars for a powerful seal. What does the seal contain or hold back, and will the eldamon’s journey with their new friend help strengthen the seal or risk breaking it?
31–33The eldamon once knew the location of a lost treasure or ruin. With the way their understanding of the mortal world works, can they still find it? Will they bring their new friend there?
34–36The eldamon is famous among other eldamon for something they once did or a talent they possess.
37–39The eldamon was once venerated as a god or protector by a community.
40–42The eldamon was once an eldamon of another element but somehow shifted to their current element and form over time.
43–45The eldamon lost all memory of an essential event in their life.
46–48The eldamon somehow always seems to attract the attention and influence of an element different than their own.
49–51The eldamon once lived in a far-off land
52–54The eldamon has a particular snack or other guilty pleasure from the mortal world, and they’d love to have an eldamon trainer phase them in so they can indulge it.
55–57On rare occasions and circumstances that can’t be intentionally triggered, the eldamon opens a miniature temporary portal to an associated plane.
58–60The eldamon knows the deepest secret of an important figure, though given the way interact with the world and their memories, they likely have no idea the information is important.
61–63The eldamon sneezes uncontrollably when it encounters one of the elements to which it’s weak, almost as if it was allergic.
64–66The eldamon was once a creature other than an eldamon that was reborn or transformed into an eldamon by an extremely unusual circumstance.
67–69The eldamon is secretly a spy, gathering information for another original mortal friend. The spy might come to care more deeply for their new mortal friend and refuse to report, and even if they don’t, due to an eldamon’s thought patterns and memory, the original mortal friend doesn’t expect much to come from their spy.
70–72The eldamon is targeted by an evil organization trying to capture them for an unknown reason.
73–75The eldamon is obsessed with a specific form of obscure lore and wants to engage with their obsession whenever possible.
76–78The eldamon claims some worldly title in the mortal world with no real evidence, such as claiming to be the mayor of a far-off town whose name no one recognizes.
79–81The eldamon is often tired, and when it enters a restful state, it usually accidently melds into a nearby object until it awakens. This can be a boon or bane depending on the eldamon and the object.
82–84The eldamon hoards tokens related to their element, and hides them among their mortal friend’s belongings.
85–87This eldamon came from the elemental energies of a different world or reality—not the same world as other eldamon and their mortal friend.
88–90The eldamon secretly watched over their mortal friend when the friend was younger.
91–93The eldamon is trying to win the affection of another eldamon, and they might bring their mortal friend in on the secret to ask for help.
94–96The eldamon goes by a nickname and keeps their true name secret.
97–100The eldamon has a secret personality quirk (roll again on Table 8) that they choose to hide. Your original roll on Table

Chapter 1: A World of Eldamon.name: 第1章：艾尔达蒙的世界 Chapter 1: A World of Eldamon

A World of Eldamon.name: 艾达蒙的世界 A World of Eldamon
A World of Eldamon.text:
What are eldamon? That's a good question! It depends on who you ask, but I prefer the explanation given in the popular essay "Eldamon: Elements of Creation," which I've enclosed below. The author doesn't manage to settle on a single definition, but I feel they do the best job at describing the facts and letting you, the reader, come to your own conclusion. If you want to know more about some of the topics mentioned in the essay, I've enclosed some additional sources for your perusal. Good luck, and welcome to the world of eldamon!

Eldamon are strange elemental creatures that live hidden in the world's untamed places, waiting for daring explorers to find and befriend them. They exist across the world and can inhabit nearly any sort of space or location, but they're frequently in hiding. The majority of eldamon are content to simply coexist with their surroundings, but a select few emerge to engage with the outside world, and those eldamon are just waiting for daring adventurers searching for friends.

But what exactly are eldamon, and where do they come from? This is a question that scholars and theologians have discussed, analyzed, and theorized upon for ages, with no single consensus. Eldamon are seemingly immortal elemental beings as diverse and varied as the world itself, with researchers uncovering, befriending, and cataloging new types of eldamon on a regular basis. While some individual eldamon are quite young, eldamon as a whole are ancient, and some theories consider eldamon to be a part of the creation of the universe or perhaps one of the aftereffects of that process. When most people think of an ancient creature, they might imagine a being so distant and aloof that they don't deign to interact with mortal beings, but eldamon defy that assumption. Scholars believe that an eldamon's ability to connect with ephemeral mortals is tied to their unique perspective on the world and the passing of time. After years of discussion, research, and analysis, the consensus opinion is that most eldamon live in the moment without focusing much on history. This leads many eldamon to possess an almost childlike nature and view of the world, even though ancient eldamon might be thousands, millions, or even billions of years old.

One aspect that all eldamon share in common is that each is tied to one of twenty specific elements found within the world, the twenty elements revered by the elemental religion known as the Eld. These elements range from the concrete and physical, such as air, earth, fire, water, and wood, to others that might seem a bit abstract or esoteric, such as life, death, space, time, or music. Elementalists who don't follow the Eld sometimes disagree with the use of the term "element" to describe some of these twenty sources of power for eldamon, but the term has been used this way since ancient times, so attempts by these scholars and theorists to change the terminology are unlikely to succeed.

Each eldamon is always tied to exactly one element, and specific types of eldamon can often be found around or melded into examples of that element in the natural world. A pair of unsolved questions that researchers would dearly love to answer is why certain types of eldamon came to exist and embody specific elements, and why some of those eldamon are more common than others. Why is a squirrel-like form the most common manifestation of the element of fire? Few things in the natural world connect fire and squirrels. Eldamon forms occasionally match a researcher's "common sense," such as water eldamon shaped like aquatic creatures and air eldamon shaped like flying creatures, but they also sometimes defy such expectations, such as sun lions, fire elephants, and snow otters, just to name a few. While there are dozens of theories, there are three main categories that researchers use to divide the theories.

Essentialists believe that there is something essential about these forms that led to their adoption by eldamon—to an essentialist, the form is the original, and if it resembles an animal, then the animal might have come to exist because of the eldamon and not vice versa. The trouble with the essentialist perspective is that it's difficult to prove, but it remains popular with those who view eldamon and their forms as unlikely to be influenced by the mortal world rather than vice versa.

Impressionists believe that ancient eldamon were impressionable and malleable in form and that they came to resemble creatures and objects that left a strong impression until, over time, they locked in a form. Meanwhile, impressionists believe that newborn eldamon derive their forms from impressions of older eldamon—in essence, the most populous eldamon continue to be the most common since it's more likely for newborn eldamon to come across a common eldamon than a rare one.

Lastly, narrativists believe that the shared stories and culture of mortalkind shaped eldamon. By telling stories of a possum seeking to cast the world in eternal darkness, mortalkind encourages darkness eldamon to take the form of a possum. People who believe in narrativist theories say that their view can explain eldamon forms and commonality the most elegantly of the three main branches, since they don't need to fall back on an unknown "essence" or the circular definition that being popular leads to being more popular. Detractors feel that narrativists are too focused on a mortal-centric viewpoint of the universe, giving their own roles outsized importance by promoting the power of mortal imagination and storytelling.

Whatever the reason for taking the forms they do, another aspect that all eldamon have in common is their ability to transform their form and appearance into a few variations to fit their circumstances. Typically, most eldamon found in the wild appear as a tiny and unassuming form that most eldamon scholars call an eldamon's "pet form." This nickname for their default form comes from their cute and endearing appearance; it doesn't mean that an eldamon discovered in this form is literally someone's pet. Like many aspects of eldamon, this naming convention has been used for so long that the origin has been lost to time and has simply become the default name for an eldamon in this form.

While their default form is typically innocent in appearance, eldamon are powerful and relatively intelligent elemental beings. Most eldamon are peaceful and playful beings, especially when in their pet forms, but like any creature, they can become agitated. When this occurs or when an eldamon wishes to tussle with other eldamon, they can transform their bodies into larger, stronger, and combat-ready battle forms. While in battle form, eldamon often fight other eldamon; however, these sorts of "fights" occur simply for play or to settle a bet, and the result of such battles is that the loser is de-energized for a time but never permanently harmed. Although mostly harmless to creatures of the mortal world, these fights can be truly spectacular to witness, as eldamon in these forms unleash their elemental powers, ancient magical abilities imbued into their very being. Although these battles can seem intense when viewed from a mortal perspective, eldamon seem to enjoy them as a type of play, similar to how puppies wrestle and play-fight as a form of socialization. Neither eldamon is harmed in the end, and the battles themselves are quite a sight to behold.

But these two forms aren't the only ones an eldamon can take. Occasionally, when an eldamon learns to become deeply in tune with their element, they can enter into their rarely-seen evolved form, a more powerful version of their battle form. Only particularly special eldamon, or those who have formed a strong bond with their inner elemental powers, can evolve from their battle form into this impressive evolved form. These forms are usually larger and more powerful than a normal battle form, and they possess a special ability unique to the evolved eldamon. Your first time seeing an evolved eldamon is something you'll never forget. Will it stir wonder and excitement in your heart, or sheer awe?

Although these forms sound dangerous, eldamon, by nature, are playful creatures of elemental energy that exist out of phase with our sense of reality throughout the natural world, especially in places with concentrations of their associated type of elemental energy. Being out of phase means they can only directly affect or be affected by other out-of-phase beings (usually other eldamon). However, they are still visible and capable of causing indirect effects over time, either due to their ambient energy or by melding into creatures, objects, or locations that interest them. That is the most likely explanation for why eldamon sometimes act in ways that seem alien to us; they literally exist in a world of their own!

Another interesting aspect of eldamon is that, due to their playful nature, nearly anyone can befriend an eldamon and travel alongside them. When they're active, rather than melded into their environment, eldamon love companionship, and most possess a natural affinity to bond with sapient beings. It's not unheard of for people to form bonds with dozens, if not hundreds, of eldamon at a time. While most people are happy to befriend a single eldamon, or none at all.

However, only those who dedicate their lives to bonding with eldamon and understanding their mysterious ways can help unlock the depths of an eldamon's potential. These eldamon trainers devote their entire being to their connection to their eldamon friends—to a supernatural degree. Eldamon trainers can help train their eldamon allies to reach greater heights than either could alone (hence the name "trainer"), but their metaphysical connection is even more profound. Through this connection and training with their eldamon, trainers can exchange their phase with their bonded eldamon, allowing the eldamon to enter our world while the trainer phases out. This exchange allows the eldamon to affect our world directly with their elemental powers for a limited time and coordinate with the trainer. Trainers also help their eldamon tap into their inner elemental powers in order to evolve into their most powerful form.

Finally, the last commonly-known aspect of eldamon is that they are beings of pure elemental energy, and when they aren't traveling around, they often meld into creatures, objects, or locations that suit them. And if an eldamon stays melded into an object for a long enough time, this process can grant elemental powers to that creature, object, or location. The most extreme examples of this phenomenon are elemental avatars, who deeply bond with the eldamon within, thereby gaining access to all the eldamon's elemental powers.

By understanding the ways eldamon interact with their environment and affect our everyday lives, we can begin to appreciate just how important they are to us as well as to the world around us. And with so many mysteries still unsolved, there's only one option—go forth into the world and explore, seeking out new eldamon and new answers, so that you can write your own name in the history books!

Quickstart Guide for Players

As a player, the meat of the content you're looking for is likely Chapter 2: the eldamon trainer and elemental avatar classes; Chapter 3: the elemental powers for each of the elements; and Chapter 4: the eldamon entries for your current eldamon. But you won't need to read all of that to get started.

The classes, especially the elemental avatar, are much simpler to play and understand once you have your character sorted. There are hundreds of elemental powers, but you only need to look at around four to build and play your first elemental avatar character. A 1st-level elemental avatar has their powers more or less locked in once you choose an element, so pick an element that inspires you, or that matches your personality or playstyle (see the radar charts on page 71 for ratings).

Air, death, fire, and mind are all good starting point elements for an easier learning curve—especially air and fire. So if you'd like a little less complexity, you might choose one of those elements as an elemental avatar or choose eldamon from those elements to start as an eldamon trainer. For an eldamon trainer, you can find all the powers you will require in the eldamon entries, so you won't need to learn all the powers and choose from them.

Even if you're not a trainer, you might want to collect and befriend eldamon as part of your character's story. Either way, ask your GM how they'd prefer to handle finding and befriending eldamon, as there are ways to manage this. If your GM asks you to control your eldamon progression so they don't have to add another component to their prep work, you should read the section on befriending eldamon and familiarize yourself with the eldamon entries in Chapter 4.

Character Creation: Step-by-Step

The following guide allows you to quickly and easily build a 1st-level character of either of the classes from this book without needing to read more than a few pages first before you can get started and play, learning the rest as you go.

Step 1: Choose Your Class

First, decide if you want to play an eldamon trainer, finding and befriending as many eldamon as you can and sending them in to fight in your place, or an elemental avatar, embodying and mastering your chosen element.

Step 2 for Elemental Avatar: Choose Your Element

Based on either the radar charts, your personal preferences, or both, choose one of the 13 elements. If you can't decide, consider fire as your first element.

Step 2 for Eldamon Trainer: Choose Your Eldamon

Choose your three starting eldamon from among the common eldamon. The eldamon trainer class has some suggested starting line-ups, but if you can't decide, consider squirrelash, kitopaz, and morpup.

Step 3: Find Your Powers

If you're an elemental avatar, you have all the 1st-level powers of your element (active, reactive, and refresh). If you're an eldamon trainer, your eldamon have the powers listed in their eldamon entries in Chapter 4. You don't have to look at any of the other powers. It might be helpful to write your powers' names, and potentially some reminder text, on notecards so you can lay them out as you use them and pick them up when you refresh them. You can also use Eldamon Battles cards for this purpose.

Step 4: Finalize Your Character

Head to your class's write-up (eldamon trainer or elemental avatar) and read the statistics for a starting character, then apply them. As an eldamon trainer, you'll need to choose your calling, whereas for an elemental avatar, you've already chosen your element in step 2.

Quickstart Guide for GMs

As the GM, you have a lot of options when adding eldamon to your game. If you want to go deep with eldamon lore, you should pay close attention to this chapter. Read the various suggestions and variants, make decisions for your game, and then use the method for befriending eldamon that works best for you, with you in the driver's seat.

On the far opposite side of the spectrum, if you're reading this book because your player is a huge eldamon fan who handed it to you and you're wondering, "Am I going to have to redo the whole campaign for this character or now have to add eldamon throughout my game?" the answer is no! You only have to look at the assumptions of eldamon (see below) and decide how eldamon best fit into your world. Then, briefly glance at the options for befriending eldamon and choose one method to run yourself or assign it to the player who loves eldamon.

Even if you don't choose the simplest option for befriending eldamon—where the player just picks a few eldamon when leveling up their character—the system has tables that your player can use to independently generate eldamon without requiring prep or responses from you. Of course, you might be somewhere in between, so you can delegate as much or as little to the players as you wish!

Eldamon Assumptions

There's no need for your group's game to follow all the default assumptions regarding eldamon. Depending on your group's preferred setting, some or all of them might not apply. Thus, before we dig deeper into the specifics of the default world of eldamon, let's examine the two major eldamon assumptions. First, eldamon are ever-present but elusive and phased out, and second, eldamon are known and visible.

Assumption 1: Eldamon are Present Everywhere but Elusive and Phased Out

In the default setting of Battlezoo Eldamon, you can find eldamon just about anywhere interesting (especially if there's a lot of elemental energy, but even if not). Still, they tend to be elusive, hiding throughout the world. Other than some ancient eldamon that might be able to phase in at will, most eldamon are phased out and often melded into creatures, objects, or locations, all but making them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} while they are melded into other items.

But what does that mean? By default, being phased out doesn't imply an eldamon travels to another plane; instead, an eldamon is coterminous with the mortal world, able to see the world and be seen, but usually unable to physically interact (except in very specific ways). Eldamon trainers can also become phased out, but mortal creatures weren't built to live their lives that way. Eldamon exist nearly their entire lives phased out as it's their natural state, while mortal creatures normally can't become phased out at all. As a result, trainers are extremely limited in their actions when phased out, and they usually can't act at all. Meanwhile, eldamon still have access to all of their powers and abilities in either state, though they much prefer being phased out whenever possible.

A phased-out eldamon tends to meld into creatures, objects, and environments that interest them, essentially merging with them and possibly granting their host an elemental ability or power. Most eldamon are melded at any given time, and since there's not an easy way to detect a melded eldamon unless they choose to end the meld and make themself known, this makes eldamon all the more elusive to those who search for them. While melded, an eldamon might hibernate for extended periods, or they might stay active and watch what's going on from their snug and comfy spot. Because the GM (or player) might not determine what eldamon are present until after an encounter is over, it's not mandatory to add a special effect when an eldamon is melded into a creature, object, or environment; often, it takes time for such synergies to happen, and the object and the eldamon have to be compatible with each other before such powers can appear. However, a GM who rolls for eldamon in advance can use them as an inspiration for fun additional elemental effects on creatures, magic items, or environments (for environments, they could represent a notable effect on the battlefield or even an environmental hazard).

For example, a pre-planned creature might have melded with a fire eldamon, allowing it to gain a fire elemental power that it can use once during a battle. Incidentally, what's good for the opposition is also good for the PCs: this same metaphysical concept is the insetting explanation for the elemental avatar class!

Assumption 2: People Know About Eldamon and Can See Them

In the default setting, almost everyone knows that eldamon exist, and anyone can see them when they aren't melded into something. The existence of eldamon throughout the world creates ripple effects throughout society. The Eld religion worships the twenty eldamon elements and venerates eldamon as a manifestation of these elements. People on the streets of a city, town, or village won't usually look askance at someone being followed by a cadre of eldamon friends. In fact, the villagers might have eldamon friends of their own!

In addition to companionship, other folk befriend eldamon and train them to participate in competitive battles while out of phase, sometimes even in public tournaments. Since most eldamon enjoy these competitions as a way to play with other eldamon, and since they aren't genuinely harmed in battle (they aren't flesh and blood creatures, so at worst, they lose some elemental energy and need to rest after a battle), fans of eldamon battles can enjoy the spectacle of amazing explosions, lightning bolts, and falling meteors without the guilt of most arena combat. And since a phased-out eldamon can't affect the mortal world, spectators aren't at risk of harm during these battles.

Though only eldamon trainers can phase an eldamon into the material world to fight against their shared foes, any character, not just an eldamon trainer, can befriend an eldamon and have their eldamon friends battle each other while phased out (assuming the eldamon are willing).

But your world isn't necessarily the default Battlezoo world of Alacar, so you might tweak these assumptions to fit the story of your setting. Whatever you choose, eldamon provides a wealth of lore and mechanics to back you up. To get you started, consider these three variations:

  • Eldamon as Invisible Spirits: Eldamon are still present in the world, but they're mysterious and worshiped individually as local spirits. Only spirit seers can see them when they're phased out. It's usually best to allow all PCs who want to befriend eldamon to be spirit seers for free. If you want to be stricter, being a spirit seer could be a rare background that grants Wisdom or Charisma, one free attribute boost, and the ability to see eldamon and other similar spirits; you could also add a general feat that grants the ability to see eldamon and other similar spirits, but most characters can't gain a general feat until level 3. If you choose the stricter option, eldamon trainers will need to be spirit seers, so you should probably give them the ability for free so that they don't need to spend their background on it. Because eldamon can have metaphysical effects or promote the energies of their element, spirit seers who can see them, communicate with them, and resolve issues involving them are in high demand.
  • Eldamon as Extraplanar Beings: In a setting with this assumption, eldamon live on some other plane of existence (an appropriate Elemental Plane or other similar place, such as the Astral Plane for mind eldamon and the Dimension of Time for time eldamon). Eldamon trainers call them from their home into a liminal twilight realm between spaces, with the same mechanical effects of an out-of-phase eldamon. Otherwise, characters rarely, if ever, come across eldamon in the world around them. This setting requires the fewest adjustments by the GM and works well with an automatic variant of befriending eldamon, with the idea that the trainer conjures the automatic eldamon from their home plane into the realm between spaces rather than finding them during their adventure.
  • Eldamon as Physical Creatures: A setting where eldamon are physical creatures in the natural world is great for those who wish to embrace the world of eldamon throughout their world entirely. In such a place, whole societies might use eldamon elemental energies to perform daily tasks and derive power, like a squirrelash-powered oven or a chillot-powered freezer. Meanwhile, the world's ecology might be dominated by eldamon and their elemental powers. Given how vital the phase mechanic is to the eldamon trainer class, you might need to develop a new explanation of how the class works. Perhaps they bond with their eldamon in an unusual way, similar to a summoner and eidolon, and that's the only way to have eldamon join a character on their adventure and fight for them.

If you retain the idea that anyone can have eldamon join them on an adventure, and they aren't phased out, you'll wind up with numerous mechanical questions related to hazards, area effects, and so on, so take that into account (or decide to handwave it in advance). In addition, if you treat eldamon as living creatures, they might be hurt and die in battle, which could lead to some mechanical differences as well as ethical questions if you still have eldamon battles in the setting. This variation is potentially exciting when it comes to worldbuilding but will probably require the most work by the GM to make this option work well within your setting.

Eldamon Ecology

Eldamon might appear cute in their pet form, but they're elemental beings, and their concept of reality is far different from that of other creatures. Thus, they act in ways most wouldn't expect, especially when comparing them to the creatures they most resemble in the natural world. While eldamon have an intellect roughly on par with mortal beings, their thought process and perspective are so distinctive that it's hard to use them as a source of scouting or hard facts, especially while they're phased out and even more detached from the physical workings of the mortal world. Even so, eldamon can be excellent conversationalists with mortals, assuming they share a language, and discuss topics that interest them; they're just often filled with unusual details that feel important or salient to the eldamon but aren't necessarily as interesting or immediately applicable to their mortal conversation partner.

Similarly, while exploring the world, eldamon friends follow along with their mortal friends, safely phased out. The difference between an eldamon trainer and other mortals is that the trainer can phase out and have their eldamon friends phase in to help out during combat, and a trainer can help an eldamon evolve to their highest form. Either way, these phased-out eldamon generally follow along with their friends, paying attention to their own whims and interests, without providing commentary useful to scouting the area. For instance, a phased-out squirrelash might find something it wants to collect, but that should be unlikely to be a plot-important @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} item that the rest of the party failed to find. Think of the phased-out eldamon following around (possibly dozens of them) as a potential for the occasional fun roleplay hook as opposed to a way to exploit dozens and dozens of relevant checks during exploration. That said, eldamon aren't guaranteed to always keep their noses out of anything plot-relevant. The GM might decide to have the object of a squirrelash's fascination trigger the start of a new adventure or even use a phased-out eldamon to help prod the group if they get stuck. But the default assumption is that they don't contribute much directly during exploration unless the player is an eldamon trainer with the training to phase their eldamon in to use their elemental assistance ability, a skill action, a standard exploration activity, or some other eldamon ability to assist the party.

While eldamon following around their mortal friends don't have a significant effect on the world around them during an adventure as they head to so many new locations, lone eldamon can create elemental effects in an area over time (especially if they meld into the environment), affecting the location in subtle or overt ways. In the same way that even a phased-out eldamon can come in for a cuddle with their trainer, a lone eldamon can eventually interact with certain parts of the mortal world that heavily connect to their preferences despite their lack of phase. For instance, a lone squirrelash can manage to touch, grab, and gather tiny objects in our world for their collection.

Phase

By default, an eldamon's phase is a state of existence coterminous with the mortal world, allowing eldamon to see, hear, and speak to beings and objects in the mortal world but not to directly touch, harm, or affect them (and vice versa). In some ways, this is similar to creatures looking onto the Material Plane from the Ethereal Plane, but no planar travel is involved, and unlike ethereal creatures, phased-out creatures are visible from the Material Plane. Another key difference is that there are some effects that cross the veil and affect ethereal creatures, but effects from the mortal world never affect a phased-out creature, and vice versa. For example, a phased-out creature can see a medusa in the mortal world, but wouldn't be affected by the medusa's gaze.

An eldamon's natural state is being phased out, but mortal creatures usually can't phase out in the same way. Eldamon trainers have the unique capacity to phase out but must do so in exchange for phasing in their eldamon, and their physical and metaphysical bodies react to the experience in an unusual way. A phased-out eldamon trainer can't act, except in limited ways, as they're forced into an unphased state not meant for a physical creature. However, since they've phased out of their physical body, they don't need to eat, drink, or even breathe while phased out. They don't physically require sleep (and are rarely ever phased out long enough that this becomes an issue in the first place, especially since eldamon don't appreciate being phased in for that long), but an eldamon trainer who was somehow stuck phased out for an extended period of time needs to enter a quiescent state similar to sleep due to mental fatigue. Some eldamon scholars believe that eldamon trainers don't even age while phased out, but thus far, no trainer has been phased out long enough to prove it. And no eldamon trainer capable of forming a bond with their eldamon friends would willingly advance this research by intentionally having their eldamon friend phase in and then petrifying them for years or decades (not to mention, this would leave the trainer languishing in phased-out limbo for all that time).

Melding Effects

When an eldamon melds into a creature, object, or environment, there often isn't any effect, especially not at first. However, depending on their compatibility and how long the eldamon remains melded, they can produce a synergistic effect, even to the extent of transforming an extremely compatible creature into an elemental avatar!

While none of these effects is mandatory, and they usually wouldn't be possible for a group that determined the presence of eldamon on the fly, they can add a lot of flavor to campaigns where the GM rolls for eldamon in advance during session prep, or from the flip side, they provide a valuable means to adjust creatures, treasures, or environments to add elemental effects.

Creatures

While few creatures are compatible enough with an eldamon to become elemental avatars, they still might exhibit the ability to use a single limited elemental power. Either replace one of the creature's special abilities with a single elemental power of the appropriate element of the creature's level or lower, usable with a frequency of once per minute, or use the following simple adjustment for a creature with a larger connection. The adjustment also serves as a great way to give a creature elemental features.

Eldamon Infused Creature Adjustments

When a creature is infused more deeply by an eldamon, they gain abilities associated with the eldamon's element. First, choose an eldamon to infuse the creature, and note that eldamon's element (alternatively, you can choose an element without choosing an eldamon). Next, apply the following adjustments to the creature's statistics.

  • Increase the creature's level by 1.
  • Increase the creature's AC, attack modifiers, DCs, saving throws, Perception, and skill modifiers by 1.
  • Increase the damage of its Strikes and other offensive abilities by 1. If the creature has limits on how many times or how often it can use an ability (such as a spellcaster's spells or a dragon's Breath Weapon), increase the damage by 2 instead.
  • Add one elemental power of the appropriate element, of the creature's level or lower, usable with a frequency of once per minute (this is often one of the powers the eldamon knows). Additionally, add the appropriate elemental locomotion, elemental manipulation, elemental resistance, and elemental senses benefit. If the creature is at least 6th level after applying this adjustment, it also gains the enhanced locomotion ability.
  • Increase the creature's Hit Points and grant based on its starting level (see the table below).
Starting LevelHP Increase
1 or lower5

Items

Items with high compatibility with a melded eldamon can eventually develop magical properties related to the eldamon's element, which might (or might not) remain after the eldamon leaves. While a minor connection might lead to an item gaining the eldamon's resistances and weaknesses or some unusual magic item quirks related to the eldamon or its personality, more major effects are possible. For instance, an item might become a relic, with an aspect corresponding to the eldamon's element, or if it's a weapon, the item might become imbued with an imbued property corresponding to the eldamon's element (you can find the imbued properties corresponding to the thirteen elements in this book in either Battlezoo Bestiary or Battlezoo Bestiary: Strange & Unusual, and the remaining seven elements in Battlezoo Bestiary: Elemental Storm). In either case, the eldamon's level determines the maximum possible level for the relic's gifts or the imbued property, though in many cases, an eldamon doesn't provide a benefit corresponding to its full level unless it's resided within the item for a long time.

Environments

Eldamon who meld into a compatible environment sometimes begin to produce effects after long enough. This can be as simple as difficult or hazardous terrain, but the presence of a powerful eldamon can also affect ley lines and produce ley line effects, or create an area of pervasive magic where the effects of their element are enhanced. Lastly, an eldamon's presence might inadvertently lead to an environmental hazard related to their element, making the location dangerous for others to explore!

Phased In Eldamon as Creatures or NPCs

In the default setting, certain ancient eldamon can phase themselves in, potentially allowing you to use them as creatures during an encounter, but depending on what setting assumptions you choose, eldamon might be physically present all the time.

Eldamon have fewer Hit Points than expected for a creature of their level, and they don't offer as much customization as a PC, so players would likely be better off playing an elemental avatar. Still, there's nothing that would cause any problems with a permanently phased in eldamon as a PC if a player doesn't want to have to choose feats and the like; you could increase an eldamon's Hit Points per level by 4 in that circumstance.

While they're a little straightforward for most players to use as a PC, that same property makes eldamon amazing tools for a GM who wants a quick, memorable NPC or creature, either for an encounter or to work together with the PCs as allies, that can use a fun set of elemental abilities. Because eldamon have a full progression of statistics and powers for all levels, you can quickly use any of the eldamon in this book, from level 1 to 20, without having to make any decisions right out of the box (possibly increasing their Hit Points by 4 per level if they'll be a full creature on their own), essentially giving you 1,080 statblocks you can use however you like!

Creating a Custom Eldamon

Sometimes, you might want to create a unique eldamon for your campaign world or as a part of your story. Perhaps you want to port over one or more of your favorite elemental creatures from other media as an eldamon. Depending on how much time you have, you can create a new custom eldamon either quickly and easily or more thoroughly using the methods below. Using the quick method, you can make a brand new eldamon in just a few minutes! While the full method could take an hour (or longer if you draw three pictures of the eldamon, one for each stage), a significant portion of that time is spent choosing a default power progression. However, if you're making a special eldamon to use once in your game, it might not be necessary to include a default power progression, especially if you know your players are the type to customize their power progression anyway.

  • Quick Method: For a fast and easy new eldamon, come up with an appearance, common name, and preference linked to the eldamon's evolution, then find another eldamon of the same element that's most similar and start by copying its statblock. You'll likely determine that the form ability and evolution ability still don't fit, so try to choose a form and evolution ability you can use from other existing eldamon. You can write brand new abilities for one or both if necessary.
  • Full Method: Using the existing eldamon of the same element as references, go through step by step and choose each mechanical and narrative aspect of your new eldamon. Try to have the eldamon's abilities work together in interesting ways, and select a default power progression that makes the most of those abilities (for instance, if your new eldamon has an elemental ability that works on area effects, be sure to take plenty of area powers). Consider drawing your eldamon's forms or commissioning some art of your eldamon (if it's based on an elemental creature from other media, you might have pictures already) and using the pictures as a basis for the form ability and the evolution ability. Pictures can add a lot to your eldamon, so they are often worth the time and effort, and your players will surely appreciate it! If you're inspired to do so, you can even write up your own eldadex entries for the new eldamon!

Eldamon Battles

Eldamon are especially popular in the default setting partly because of the entertainment value of eldamon battles: they are colorful and flashy, with twists and turns and various visually appealing techniques. Plus, many eldamon enjoy battles as a type of play and aren't hurt; they're just de-energized and need to rest. This makes eldamon battles more interesting and humane than other arena combat. Due to the popularity of eldamon battles, hundreds of different rulesets exist for eldamon battles, whether they're fought on the road between two adventuring eldamon trainers, among friends and their eldamon companions in a nearby field, or even in a public exhibition match at a huge arena among professionals.

Battles for Fun and Profit

Throughout the world of Alacar, most cities and towns have rudimentary arenas for eldamon battles, where locals can battle their eldamons against friends, visitors, or even family. These arenas can be as simple as a patch of grass with marks and chalk or more complex with stands for spectators. Most battles are fought in these home-grown arenas, but even a small patch of dirt or grass will do for friendly matches.

However, there are also professional eldamon leagues and competitions throughout Alacar, where one can find some of the most impressive arenas in the world. These arenas are designed to hold thousands of spectators and have elemental fields built into them that allow for environmental effects that can change the rules on the fly. These professional bouts often draw massive crowds and allow for longer and more varied tournaments. Any rules that require an elemental field list "arena only" in parentheses.

No matter your ruleset, eldamon battles adhere to the format outlined below. There are virtually no exceptions, and such a strange ruleset would be hard to recognize as an eldamon battle at all.

Step 1: Choose Venue, Stakes, and Ruleset

Before beginning an eldamon battle, you must agree on when, where, and how it will happen. There's a big difference between a friendly eldamon battle in your backyard just for practice and an epic showdown in a massive coliseum with the championship on the line.

Some rulesets don't work well outside the elemental fields found in professional eldamon arenas, so deciding on the venue first is most important. While many possible battlefields exist, for officially ranked eldamon battles, the standard arena is shaped like a circle with a 150-foot radius, and eldamon typically start either 30 or 60 feet apart, depending on either the consent of both contestants or a coin toss if they disagree. See @UUID[.SEAPdEgNoIphZoHM#menu-a:-starting-position]{Menu A: Starting Position} for additional considerations on the location's setup.

Most eldamon battles are just for fun, but when stakes are involved, tension is higher, so it's essential to settle the venue, stakes, and ruleset in advance.

The ruleset choice will determine how every other step proceeds, so choosing a ruleset is necessary once you know the venue and stakes before continuing. By selecting a ruleset, you decide which rules to use from the Ruleset Menus, and if you're in an eldamon arena, you can choose whether to employ the @UUID[.SEAPdEgNoIphZoHM#optional-arena-rule:-faster-recovery]{optional arena rules}.

Step 2: Choose Eldamon

Once you've determined how the battle will proceed, it's time to choose your eldamon. The ruleset determines how many eldamon are battling at once (@UUID[.SEAPdEgNoIphZoHM#menu-b:-eldamon-fielded]{Menu B}) and how many total eldamon you can bring over the course of the battle (@UUID[.SEAPdEgNoIphZoHM#menu-c:-team-size]{Menu C}). Officially ranked eldamon battles include four brackets: solo, pair, 1v1 team, and 2v2 team. In the solo format, you choose one eldamon for your team; in the pair format, you select two (who both battle at once); for the 1v1 team format, you choose six eldamon, battling one at a time; and in the 2v2 team format, you select six eldamon, each battling two at a time. In officially ranked matches, teams are chosen in secret and only revealed once each team is ready, but some formats have teams alternately choosing eldamon so that they can respond to what they know about the other team. For these types of matches, one common implementation is that the first team chooses one eldamon, then the second team chooses two eldamon, then the first team chooses two eldamon, and so on. This way the second team isn't always just responding to the first team's selections, and with an even number of eldamon, the team forced to choose first (a disadvantage, as they have no information about the other team) also gets to choose last (an advantage, as they can slip in one eldamon at the end without the other team having any way to respond).

Step 3: Battle!

Eldamon battles proceed for the most part using the game's standard combat rules, with every eldamon using their statistics for their level from their eldamon entry. The fight continues until an eldamon is defeated, an eldamon forfeits, or one of the teams chooses to substitute for another eldamon (assuming a format where a team has more eldamon than the number fielded at once). A defeated eldamon doesn't necessarily mean a match is over, especially when multiple eldamon are involved in the battle. If a team has more eldamon ready to fight, their next eldamon enters the arena (see substitution below).

Step 4: Substitution

A defeated eldamon (an eldamon reduced to 0 Hit Points, or who is defeated in a different way if you're using a variant from @UUID[.SEAPdEgNoIphZoHM#menu-d:-defeating-eldamon]{Menu D}) or an eldamon who forfeits the match can substitute in for another eldamon if their team has more eldamon available than are currently on the field. In most formats, including officially ranked eldamon battles, a mandatory substitution due to a defeat or forfeit pauses the fight until the new eldamon can enter; this serves as a catch-up mechanic for the team that lost their eldamon; otherwise, the team that was ahead would get further ahead. In game terms, this is the same encounter, and the new eldamon acts when the old eldamon's turn would have occurred (initiative doesn't change for the new eldamon, they simply take the place of the original eldamon). However, some formats don't mind the death spiral and refuse to pause for a new eldamon after a defeat or forfeit and continue the fight, even during substitution. In game terms, this more lethal version means that the new eldamon essentially loses a turn coming onto the field, and the victorious eldamon is able to attack first.

In addition to mandatory substitutions, in most formats with additional eldamon, the team captain (usually the eldamon's mortal friend) can also call for a voluntary substitution. Usually, a voluntary substitution forces the substituting team to skip their turn as one eldamon leaves the field and another enters, granting tempo to the other team in exchange for a more favorable match-up. Nearly every eldamon battle rule that allows voluntary substitutions also limits their frequency to avoid a situation where two battlers infinitely substitute in order to gain an elemental advantage against each other. The most typical limitation is that after someone voluntarily substitutes their eldamon, they can't do so again on the next turn. Still, some rulesets are even stricter, limiting the total number of voluntary substitutions for the entire match or requiring the passage of multiple turns before an additional voluntary eldamon substitution.

Step 5: Victory Conditions

In officially ranked eldamon battles, one team wins when the other team's eldamon are all either defeated or forfeited, and there are none left to battle in the area. However, some rulesets use different rules to determine the victor, and defeating all of an opponent's eldamon is only one of many ways to win an eldamon battle (see @UUID[.SEAPdEgNoIphZoHM#menu-e:-determining-victory]{Menu E}).

Ruleset Menus and Optional Arena Rules

While the steps of an eldamon battle are almost always the same, these menus list variables that change from ruleset to ruleset, and the optional arena rules allow combatants in an eldamon arena to make significant adjustments to recovery time and elemental affinity.

Menu A: Starting Position

Many rulesets have different starting positions, which can heavily influence the course of a battle. Some elements, or even specific trainers and their eldamon, prefer a long starting distance, while others want to get up close and personal. Some of the most common starting positions are 30 feet away and 60 feet away, though they vary widely by ruleset. There might also be a maximum range, after which an eldamon has moved out of bounds and is eliminated if they moved there on purpose (force and water eldamon prefer an out-of-bounds rule that also allows them to push the other eldamon out of bounds and eliminate them, but few rulesets use this feature). Some arenas have special terrain sets to make the battlefield more exciting or even cycle between different types of terrain over time during the battle to keep combatants on their toes.
