Menu B: Eldamon Fielded

Because eldamon battles usually occur with the eldamon phased out, even characters who aren't eldamon trainers can have their eldamon friends participate in battles. This also means there isn't necessarily a metaphysical limit to how many eldamon could be fielded. However, the most popular rulesets call for 1v1 or 2v2 matchups. These are the most varied and allow for a wide range of tactics and variety throughout the battle. Even so, there is no limit to the number of eldamon fielded during a battle, such as the always-exciting battle royale, where dozens of eldamon compete in a massive free-for-all.

Menu C: Team Size

Teams often include more eldamon than the limit of eldamon battling at once. For instance, in the officially ranked 1v1 team format, a team consists of six eldamon, and each team can field only a single eldamon in the arena at a time. Multiple mortal friends can participate in a battle, each contributing a portion of their eldamon to the team's total number (for example, one friend might have three of their eldamon in the team, one friend might have two, and another one, thereby making up the team of six eldamon and three friends). This can help ensure that both teams have the same number of eldamon, so that the match is as even as possible.

Menu D: Defeating Eldamon

Typically, an eldamon fights until they reach 0 Hit Points, which is when they must rest and recharge their energy. However, some rulesets have different criteria to determine when an eldamon is eliminated from battle. Eldamon are capable of dealing enough damage in a single power that rulesets depending on eldamon being eliminated at half Hit Points or some other lesser threshold are relatively unpopular. Since eldamon can't continue fighting beyond 0 Hit Points, the rule of elimination at 0 Hit Points is as close to universal across rule sets as possible.

Menu E: Determining Victory

Final victory criteria can vary, though most battles use something similar to the officially ranked rules that declare victory by defeating all of the other team's eldamon. In some formats, you might only need to eliminate a certain percentage of the other team's eldamon or defeat their "Commander Eldamon," which is often saved for last. In all formats, a team that refuses to field any eldamon forfeits the match, so the losing team's commander has to come out sooner or later.

Menu F: Evolution

Most rulesets for eldamon battles allow an eldamon to take its evolved form if it can, but some rulesets ban evolved forms to level the playing field. Eldamon can always adopt their original battle form if they choose, even if they have the ability to enter their evolved form. They usually have few reasons to do so since their battle form lacks their powerful evolution ability. Another way some rulesets level the playing field is by limiting the number of evolved eldamon a particular team can field. For instance, one ruleset enthusiasts call "Evolved Commander" allows each team to field exactly one evolved eldamon, who serves as their commander, meaning that the team loses if the evolved eldamon is defeated. Fans of the format feel that it makes the match more exciting since the losing team will always save their best for last and put up one last desperate fight for survival. And maybe even snatch victory from the jaws of defeat if they can take down the opposing team's remaining eldamon, evolved commander included.

Optional Arena Rule: Faster Recovery

Eldamon arenas with elemental fields can allow eldamon to recover from a defeat much faster, using the elemental field to re-energize defeated eldamon in mere seconds. This makes it possible for eldamon to recover from a defeat almost instantly or in just a few minutes if a death eldamon defeated them (which would typically delay their recovery). Because of its convenience and ability to allow back-to-back tournament battles, arenas almost always use this service. From their perspective, why wouldn't you? With this option, larger tournaments, either roundrobin or elimination style, almost always occur in an arena where they don't have to wait between rounds. In areas with no arenas nearby, they might need to hold tournaments over a longer timeframe or ban death eldamon, who delay eldamon recovery, from the tournament to ensure they don't have to wait a long time between rounds. In some "hardcore" tournaments, they intentionally hold rounds back to back without allowing faster recovery, and any time an eldamon isn't ready to compete, it means their team is down an eldamon for that round. As expected, hardcore tournaments aren't for the faint of heart. In such tournaments, some contestants will even collude with each other by bringing death eldamon to weaken an opponent's lineup before their next match with a confederate.

Optional Arena Rule: Negate Resistances and Weaknesses

Some battlers lament that resistances and weaknesses can take much of the strategy out of a battle, as the eldamon with the elemental advantage wins by the brute force of their resistance and the other eldamon's weakness. It's possible to temporarily remove the eldamon resistances and weaknesses using the elemental fields at an arena to combat that. This allows for a match that tacticians feel is determined more purely by skill and training rather than elemental balance.

Befriending Eldamon.name: 与埃尔达蒙交友 Befriending Eldamon
Befriending Eldamon.text:
If you want to include eldamon in your game world, story, or character, one of your most important decisions will be how to handle the aspect of finding and befriending them. There's an entire genre of RPGs fully devoted to stories about recruiting teams of monsters, but unless you're going all-in on an eldamon campaign, chances are that your group's game has many other themes, as well as at least some players who aren't interested in spending lots of session time on eldamon. Fortunately, eldamon are designed to work well with whichever amount of focus you choose! Talk to your group and figure out which of the following methods works best for you.

Befriend Them All!

Any character, not just an eldamon trainer, can befriend an eldamon and convince the eldamon to follow them around. However, in order to gain an opportunity to do so, they have to find the eldamon first. There are several ways of handling this.

The simplest method is @UUID[.TLuVxMerEY2rwM9b#method-1:-automatic]{Method 1: Automatic}, but groups who aren't worried about adding a little complexity and who want a full monster befriending and collecting experience might prefer @UUID[.TLuVxMerEY2rwM9b#method-2:-gm-decides]{Method 2: GM Decides} or @UUID[.TLuVxMerEY2rwM9b#method-3:-roll-on-the-discovery-table]{Method 3: Roll on the Discovery Tables}. Even if your group normally uses Method 1 or Method 2 to decide when to include new eldamon, the GM can always decide to have you roll one or more of the tables from Method 3 to add some random variance to the discovery process. In either Method 2 or Method 3, once the group has determined that they found an eldamon that was curious enough to approach them, it's time to see if that eldamon is interested in traveling with one of the characters. Typically, the fact that the eldamon approached the PCs means that the eldamon was predisposed to want to interact with them. As such, it normally only requires a Diplomacy check to make a Request with a standard DC of the eldamon's level (typically the PC's level, though the GM might determine that the eldamon is the level of the item or creature in which the eldamon was melded if that is lower and have the PC train up the eldamon to their level later). Alternatively, a PC who recognizes the eldamon and knows their preferences can attempt an appropriate skill check to demonstrate a connection with the eldamon's predilections with a very easy DC of the same level.

In the case that multiple PCs in the same group want to befriend eldamon using Method 2 or 3, in order to avoid conflicts, we recommend having a group of the same kind of eldamon large enough for everyone who wants to befriend one to make an attempt and get their eldamon. Otherwise, if an extremely rare eldamon everyone wants to befriend appears and only one character can become its best friend, there could be conflicts or hurt feelings. This is especially possible if more than one player is using the eldamon mechanically, such as if the group has multiple eldamon trainers. On the other hand, a group with only one character who needs to fight alongside the eldamon mechanically could just share the same eldamon for roleplaying purposes.

Method 1: Automatic

This method is the simplest way to handle eldamon, requiring essentially no adjudication during play, but it is also often the least satisfying for fans of the monster collection subgenre since it removes the element of thrill from encountering an unexpected eldamon during a journey, getting to know them, and potentially recruiting them. In this method, a character seeking to gain an eldamon simply chooses a new eldamon when they level up. While leveling up, the character should choose between one eldamon (for a simpler character to build and play) and three eldamon (for a complex character with a lot of choices necessary to build and play) from the common eldamon and any other eldamon to which the GM gives them access. Instead of having them appear out of nowhere, the player might decide that the eldamon they chose emerged during their adventure at a time and place of their choosing. This method is easy to handle and track, and it removes the possibility of variance (bad rolls leading to no new eldamon) or elemental mismatch in a pre-published adventure where the GM doesn't want to improvise to include encounters that might match a specific element.

Method 2: GM Decides

This method is suitable for groups where the GM is deeply invested in eldamon and pre-plans their inclusion to emphasize particular themes and stories. This method also requires the most work from the GM, but it can be extremely rewarding. Essentially, when using this method, the GM adds whatever eldamon makes the most sense to the adventure in any given situation. When employing this method, the GM must remember to add and include eldamon at a steady rate. This method puts a lot of onus on the GM, so when using this method, your group might consider allowing the players to take part in the process to back up the GM. For instance, if the GM is having trouble remembering to add eldamon with this method, the players might suggest good places to include them.

For instance, if the group is in a volcano or finds a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.G3a60Ug3YNCMyMVR]{Searing Blade}, they might also recommend adding a fire eldamon. When using this method, the GM might want to roll on some of the customization tables included in method 3, after choosing the eldamon themselves, or they might select results that feel appropriate for the eldamon in the narrative without rolling.

Method 3: Roll on the Discovery Table

This method allows either the player interested in eldamon or the GM to roll on a table and determine when they find eldamon, and which ones.

When using this method, the GM must first decide roughly how many eldamon, on average, they'd like the group to encounter. "Low" works out to approximately one eldamon per level, "Standard" works out to roughly two, and "High" works out to about three. In reality, most groups will find more eldamon than the listed number using this method, potentially significantly more if they come across a large number of elemental treasure caches that trigger more rolls for eldamon or engage in trivial encounters. If the group has multiple players who want to befriend eldamon, you can either roll once and have multiple of the same kind of eldamon appear (recommended) or roll multiple times, once for each player (not recommended, as it can lead to awkward group dynamics).

Whenever the PCs enter a significant location, complete an encounter (which might or might not involve finding treasure), or discover a treasure cache unrelated to an encounter, roll on Table 1a, 1b, or 1c if the location, encounter, or treasure cache doesn't contain a creature, hazard, item, or other component or factor related to one of the eldamon elements, or on Table 2a, 2b, or 2c if it does include such a component or factor. When in doubt, roll on Table 1 (1a, 1b, or 1c as appropriate for the eldamon rate your group is using), and if determining an elemental connection proves too challenging, you can always roll on Table 1 if you wish. If more than one element applies at the same time, use Table 2, but randomly determine which element is the "related element." Even when you are in an area with a related element, there is a small chance you might find an eldamon of a different element. In that case, the eldamon might have gotten lost or be a bit unusual in their interests and might want to join you to get out or explore other areas.

Related Encounters, Items, and Locations by Element

The following are examples of where to find an eldmon of a specific element based on the types of creatures, hazards, items, and locations within the presence of a predominant element. Use these as guidelines on when you should roll on Table 2 instead of Table 1 when discovering eldamon.

  • Air: Creatures, hazards, or items with the air trait. Ambient magic with the air trait. Area or environment with extremely high winds or at extremely high altitude.

  • Body: Creatures, hazards, or items focused on enhancing the body or martial arts. Ambient magic associated with enhancing the body or martial arts. An area of martial training or practice, such as an arena, gymnasium, or dojo.

  • Darkness: Creatures, hazards, or items with the darkness or shadow traits. Ambient magic with the darkness or shadow traits. An area that's unnaturally dark (not just in a shadow or out of the light).

  • Death: Creatures, hazards, or items with the death trait. Psychopomps. Ambient magic with the death trait. A desert, badland, or other region with very little life. A place that's unnaturally dead.

  • Earth: Creatures, hazards, or items with the earth trait. Ambient magic with the earth trait. Significant earthen, rock, or crystalline features such as a huge mountain or a cavern full of crystals.

  • Electricity: Creatures, hazards, or items with the electricity trait. Ambient magic with the electricity trait. An area with electric storms, electric currents, or the like.

  • Fire: Creatures, hazards, or items with the fire trait. Ambient magic with the fire trait. Volcanoes, wildfires, or other fiery environmental conditions.

  • Force: Creatures, hazards, or items with the force trait. Ambient magic with the force trait. An area with strange forces at work.

  • Ice: Creatures, hazards, or items with the cold trait. Ambient magic with the cold trait. A frigid environment, such as a tundra.

  • Life: Creatures, hazards, or items with the vitality or healing traits. Ambient magic with the vitality or healing traits. An area with intense life force.

  • Light: Creatures, hazards, or items with the light trait. Ambient magic with the light trait. An area with intense natural lighting conditions.

  • Metal: Creatures, hazards, or items with the metal trait (not just any item made out of metal). Ambient magic with the metal trait. An area with significant metal ore deposits or that is naturally composed of metal.

  • Mind: Creatures, hazards, or items with the astral or mental trait. Ambient magic with the mental trait. An area with strong connections to the mind, such as an academy or a puzzle room in a dungeon.

  • Music: Creatures, hazards, or items with the sonic trait or that rely on music. Ambient magic with the sonic trait or that relies on music. An area closely related to music, such as a concert hall, theater, or auditorium.

  • Poison: Creatures, hazards, or items with the poison trait. Ambient magic with the poison trait. An area with intense natural poison, like the Violet Woodlands in the Indigo Isles.

  • Space: Creatures, hazards, or items with the teleportation trait. Ambient magic with the teleportation trait. An area with robust spatial anomalies or extradimensional qualities.

  • Spirit: Creatures, hazards, or items with the spirit trait. Ambient magic with the spirit trait. An area with strong spiritual presence.

  • Time: Creatures, hazards, or items with the time trait. Ambient magic with the time trait. An area with unusual temporal conditions or that is outside of its natural time.

  • Water: Creatures, hazards, or items with the water trait. Ambient magic with the water trait. A large body of water, such as a lake, river, ocean, or sea.

  • Wood: Creatures, hazards, or items with the plant or wood trait (not just any item made out of wood). Ambient magic with the plant or wood trait. The presence of significant non-creature plant life, such as in an ancient forest or a lush jungle.

Variant: Skipping the Request

In any game with monster collection, one of the worst feelings is when you finally find an ultra-rare monster, and then you don't manage to collect it. If your group feels that sting especially painfully, you might want to remove the skill check from the equation when using Method 2 or Method 3 and just have the eldamon they find automatically befriend the PCs. Assuming the group was often using eldamon preferences to target the very easy DC, this won't change the overall number of eldamon they find very much, but even with a Hero Point, you can always roll two 1s in a row and miss out on the rare eldamon. While it might feel a bit odd to have all eldamon automatically accompany the party without any check, you can just consider that there were also other eldamon who didn't choose to accompany them, and your group only spent time interacting with and discussing the ones who were already successfully recruited. The GM could even narrate with comments like, "You also find several other eldamon who are far too shy to approach you and scurry away back into the foliage," to help paint a picture of a rich world full of eldamon that don't all decide to join the party.

Eldamon Tables

The following tables assist you and your group in randomizing and customizing your eldamon experience. Here's how to use each table.

Tables @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.q5OCi0ffIv8V7rZ4]{1a} and @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.ZQnJIXtd1cP4koLm]{2a}: Roll on one of these two tables to determine whether you find an eldamon when using low eldamon frequency.

Tables @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.vw5zKvFCuw1zdyoF]{1b} and @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.fppKnPRt0uIeWpS4]{2b}: Roll on one of these two tables to determine whether you find an eldamon when using standard eldamon frequency.

Tables @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.rPgfLTCAEqklPcd1]{1c} and @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.ZsUx45Tb6v5Gq8KC]{2c}: Roll on one of these two tables to determine whether you find an eldamon when using high eldamon frequency.

Tables @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.nIgrR0ddBmNrjspB]{3a} and @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.ce4ulDXcAO9uWRDl]{3b}: Roll on Table 3a when you need to generate a random element among the 13 elements presented in this book, and Table 3b when you are required to generate a random element among all 20 eldamon elements.

Table @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.JdFxKPwFVcEvMh5q]{4}: Roll on Table 4 to determine the eldamon's rarity. Depending on the result and what eldamon sources you are using, once you've rolled on Tables 1, 2, 3, and 4, there might be only one possible eldamon at that point (for instance, squirrelash is the only common fire eldamon in this book). If there's more than one possibility, randomly determine which eldamon appears. When using only this book, you'll be deciding between two options, so you can flip a coin or roll any die, assigning odds to one eldamon and evens to the other.

Table @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.RngqBuznz59bSVJd]{5}: Roll on Table 5 to determine the eldamon's background and experience. Is the eldamon young and new to the world, ancient and inscrutable, or something in between? This table also determines what languages the eldamon speaks.

Table @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.cdQK1iv643QCSkkp]{6}: Roll on Table 6 to determine if the eldamon has a special feature that sets it apart mechanically from other eldamon of their type.

Table @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.BkHjBreTmxk9kW0l]{7}: Roll on Table 7 to determine if the eldamon's appearance is especially unusual for an eldamon of their type.

Table @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.DZJWJjtdGE9rltFW]{8}: Roll on Table 8 to determine how the eldamon's personality varies from other eldamon of their type.

Table @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.Ijt8uYkVBOLCIr6X]{9a} and @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.fzdlhXowQw4lk7dk.JournalEntryPage.NEVrfxzhsKxuSzmZ]{9b}: Roll on Tables 9a and 9b to determine if the eldamon has a secret they're hiding (or perhaps a secret they don't know, or can no longer remember). Even if the GM has the player roll on the other tables, the GM should likely roll on Tables 9a and 9b so they can think about how to integrate the secret with the ongoing campaign (and consider rerolling a secret that they know wouldn't work). If the GM doesn't want to have to adjudicate eldamon secrets, your group might decide to ignore these tables entirely.

The Eld.name: 长老 The Eld
The Eld.text:
Before the Balance came, when the world was new, the Eld was already there. What is an element? Some magical scholars limit their imaginations to earth, fire, air, and water, but to the Eld, everything could be an element. Even music, which has patterns that you can find hidden in mathematical equations all throughout nature. When we follow the teachings of the Eld, we can see these artificial divisions for what they are: purely constructs to help our small minds understand the enormity of the world around us. Magical theorists say that the elements are matter, and that things like our minds and our spirits are different, rooted in different magical traditions, but to the Eld, all of these, still, are more distant elements, cousins that, when reunited, allow us to piece together the larger whole. Few these days choose to unearth the mysteries of the Eld, for the Eld is impassive, many and unknowable. Unlike the gods of the Balance, the Eld never plays favorites, so even if you learn, even if you pray, you will be able to understand and use the power, but you won't receive divine intervention on your behalf. You'll have to learn to do it yourself. Still here, huh? Impressive. In that case, I'll let you in on some secrets, all in due time…

The Eld

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.SxhT3D9gmF7jbars]{The Eld}

The Fundamental Elements

Divine Sanctification: none

Temples: ley line nexuses, monasteries, wilderness shrines

Worshippers: druids, elementalists

Sacred Animal: all

Sacred Colors: the twenty elemental colors, or "icosachrome"

Domains: air, earth, fire, nature, water

Cleric Spells: 1st: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.zDJS8E66UI0himqV], 5th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.lpT6LotUaQPfinjj], 9th: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.jrBa9deU2ULFWvSl]

Favored Weapon: fist

Divine Attribute: Constitution or Wisdom

Divine Skill: Nature

Divine Font: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.wdA52JJnsuQWeyqz] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.rfZpqmj0AIIdkVIs]

Key Edicts: find your own path, respect the elements and their manifestations, treat others as equal under the Eld

Key Anathema: force others to follow your beliefs, corrupt or defile the elements or their manifestations, beseech the Eld for favoritism

The Fundamental Elements

The Eld is one of Alacar's oldest religions, significantly predating the coming of the Father, the Mother, and their Children of the Balance. The Eld teaches adherents to worship twenty different elemental energies, as well as the vitae, spirits, thoughtforms, and other beings that are associated with those elemental energies. Followers of the Eld don't consider these various entities to be "gods" so much as paragons of the fundamental elements.

Many believers feel that the Eld has been there long before the Balance and will be there long after the Balance is gone, and so they tend to view the deities of the Balance as more like powerful guests or children compared to the Eld.

Adherents of the Eld often engage in spiritual practices and self-reflection quietly on their own, only intended to help them find their own individual path to enlightenment. They almost never practice anything resembling an evangelical large-scale organized religion with churches that proselytize and cajole others to worship using claims that the gods will favor those who donate or act a certain way and punish those who defy them. Instead, followers of the Eld believe that, much like nature itself, the Eld never plays favorites. For their part, few of the primal divinities who act as objects of worship to adherents of the Eld bother to convert others to their following either.

As a result of this relative lack of interest in spreading the faith, The Balance had a much easier time growing and has easily overtaken the Eld in popularity, despite being a newer faith. Nevertheless, the Eld persists, growing sturdily and quietly like an oak. It remains an ancient and influential faith throughout the world of Alacar and still maintains a fair number of adherents worldwide.

What is an Element

Some magical scholars limit their imaginations to earth, fire, air, and water, but to the Eld, everything could be an element. Even music has patterns that you can find hidden in mathematical equations all throughout nature.

When we follow the teachings of the Eld, we can see these artificial divisions for what they are: purely constructed to help our small minds understand the enormity of the world around us. Magical theorists say that the elements are matter and that things like our minds and our spirits are different, rooted in different magical traditions, but to the Eld, all of these are still more distant elements, cousins that, when reunited, allow us to piece together the larger whole. The truth of reality.

Few these days choose to unearth the mysteries of the Eld, for the Eld is impassive, many, and unknowable. Unlike the gods of the Balance, the Eld never plays favorites. Even if you learn, even if you strive, and even if you pray, you will be able to understand and use the power of the elements, but you won't receive divine intervention on your behalf. You'll have to become able to do it yourself.

Organization & Hierarchy (or Lack Thereof)

Because they aren't organized in the same way as other religions, followers of the Eld don't have anything resembling a hierarchy of ranks. Those of the Eld believe that each of their fellows is to be respected for the path they have taken on their own journey, and no one's hard-earned wisdom should be ignored simply because of who they are.

Typically, the only way to gain a substantial amount of influence and acclaim among followers of the Eld is to be possessed of great wisdom and keep to one's own path to enlightenment, as well as (paradoxically) to avoid seeking such influence and acclaim in the first place. This contradiction leads to many situations where the person respected as a de facto leader among a group of Eld adherents is a humble, quiet soul who serves to aid others rather than self-aggrandize or rule over them. And frankly, Eld followers wouldn't have it any other way.

Then again, there’s no expectation among followers of the Eld that one must lead simply because your peers desire you to do so. More than a few of the most respected clergy of the Eld flatly refuse all leadership roles and decision-making, going so far as to partake in a lengthy journey to distant lands in order to avoid further requests in cases where their fellows’ persistence becomes too incessant.

Places of Worship

When you come across a place where elemental ley lines meet together, forming a nexus, or where a sort of quiet air of power and reverence thrums somewhere deep within your soul, chances are you might have found one of the hidden places of the Eld scattered throughout the world, and there might even be a shrine or waymarker left by another follower of the Eld who discovered the spot long before you. The religion is so ancient that sometimes these places go unnoticed for so long that the signs from the previous visitor might be centuries old.

When a mystical location tied to elemental energy becomes well-known, followers of the Eld occasionally establish a monastery or other collective presence there. These edifices tend to be humble buildings that remain close to nature, rather than gaudy churches or cathedrals.

Elemental Manifestations

The Eld manifests itself in places, creatures, and things all over the world. Areas of deep elemental saturation occasionally have powerful magical effects arising from the swirling magic and energy, which enterprising adherents and adventurers can channel for their own benefit or to deepen their understanding of the elements. These saturations, in turn, attract Eldamon, living manifestations of the elements, who frolic and meld into the environment, items, or creatures.

Relationship With The Balance

The Eld and Eld adherents generally get along well with the more elementally inclined deities of the Balance, Baoyan, Kuu, Mului, Than Dat, and Thongfa, and those deities' adherents. There haven't been any major conflicts between them since the Calamity of Elements, when Lilin riled her Children up. In fact, there have been many situations where they've worked together toward common goals in the past. However, individual faithful of the Balance can wind up rubbing followers of the Eld the wrong way.

The biggest point of friction with the Eld comes from those in the clergy of the Balance who attempt to claim that the Twins, Aoz and Zoa, were the ones who created the cycle of life and death for Alacar. This claim is clearly a lie, even according to the commonly held beliefs about the origin of the Balance, which tell that Tovah and Lilin, the Father and Mother, came to a world full of living people who were dying to kaiju before they birthed Aoz or Zoa. This means the cycle of life and death must have existed well in advance of the advent of the Twins, even if the Twins later began to help steer the process after they were born. Nonetheless, some worshippers of Aoz or Zoa promulgate the belief that their deities were the ones to originate all of the world's life and death. However, this claim ruffles the feathers of Eld followers, particularly those focused on the elements of life and death. Even if they're annoyed, these Eld adherents rarely do anything about it other than vocally disagree with the stance, but unfortunately, particularly hardline churches like Aoz or Zoa might use that point of disagreement as an excuse to persecute followers of the Eld. For their part, the impassive Aoz and Zoa get along well with the elemental beings of life and death and seem to have no quarrel with the Eld or its faithful.

There are sometimes other conflicts between the two religions, particularly with those Children of the Balance who have quarrelsome or controlling personalities. For instance, Mului makes heavy demands on all those who travel his waters, which can occasionally bring his worshippers into conflict with aquatic followers of the Eld who don't want to follow a particular rule or don't see any useful purpose in paying a toll to the Balance's sea deity.

The Eld and the Elements

According to the adherents of the Eld, each element has its own inner personality and tendencies, and it's best to understand them all in order to approach them in a way likely to be productive to both you and the world around you. Some adherents choose to specialize or focus on a specific element or elements, especially as they find the element synergizes with their own nature or temperament.

Some subgroups within the Eld's worshippers divide the elements into various categories, though there's none that come even close to being universally acknowledged. While pairings like light and darkness, life and death, and space and time are relatively uncontentious, and quartets like body, life, mind, and spirit have some support, those who attempt to create universal classifications are doomed to quarrel forever with other sects and branches. For instance, kragraks classify elements as dynamic (air, fire, force, light, and lightning), esoteric (mind, music, space, spirit, and time), static (darkness, death, ice, and metal), and vital (body, life, poison, wood, and water), believing that earth transcends all four classifications. Even other faithful who appreciate the kragrak classifications overall usually want to include earth as a static element, dividing into four groups of five elements, but that alteration in particular infuriates kragraks, who are themselves made of earth but highly changeable throughout their lives.

While there are few characterizations of elements on which every adherent can agree, the following are as close to consensus as a religion like the Eld can come on each element.

Air

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.P4cccKTmynPrbmEV]{Air}

The air blows freely across the lands, peregrinating without boundaries and as capricious and formless as laughter. Eld adherents see the element of air as symbolic of mobility and personal freedom. Those who seek to associate themselves with or emulate the element of air wander from place to place, rarely staying in the same bed for more than a single night. Being as free as air and always remaining in motion requires more discipline from adherents than it might seem. After all, being like air doesn't mean they can just do whatever they want; instead, to be as formless and free as air, they need to be willing to surrender their attachments in a way that some can find difficult. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of air, they are always nomadic, traveling where the winds take them.

Body

There's something deeper hidden within the flesh and matter that makes up a body, something that empowers a living creature to exceed its own limits. Followers of the Eld see the element of body as symbolic of mastery of form and being, in a way that can transform oneself into a powerful weapon. Those who seek to learn the deeper teachings of the body element often meditate to get in touch with their body and to access the strength within themselves. While those who don't understand the Eld might assume that these body adherents would be hedonists or overly focused on muscles and exercise, but in truth, they are often mindful and contemplative in their own way. They don't necessarily read up on countless esoteric topics, but they study themselves and develop extreme proprioception and understanding of their body and its movements. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of body, they tend to develop marvelous martial arts and be notable for their peak physical condition.

Darkness

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.MvsvyHS2C32WnlAr]{Darkness}

Mortals fear the unknown, and so those without darkvision fear the dark, which occludes all that might be lurking within. But despite this primal fear, Eld worshippers know that darkness isn't evil. Sometimes ignorance is bliss, and sometimes the gentlest, most merciful thing we can do is to shroud someone in a gentle cloak of darkness to escape the merciless searing of the light or the harsh unvarnished truth that can shatter a life. On the other hand, darkness is the perfect element to support skullduggery, as the element of secrecy and the hidden draws those who wish their clandestine actions to remain unknown even more often than those who seek to provide succor from harsh realities. Darkness favors neither one nor the other, but rather envelops all in that shroud of privacy. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of darkness, they value secrecy and privacy above all else, and so they respect each other's boundaries. This can lead outsiders to see their consideration for others' secrets as lacking curiosity, and at times, living under a veil of secrecy can lead to miscommunications that transparency would solve.

Death

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.eCBzMbl4COJRgcfl]{Death}

In the Eld, with its cycle of elements, death isn't the end, and nothing is final. But it does represent a state of transition, not the ending, but an ending, one which commences a new beginning. And the way to dusty death is paved with the decline of life force, of sickness and infirmity. Like the waxing and waning of the moon, so too does fractious life lead to quiet death. Death can be cruel, but it's a cruelty necessary to the cycle of the elements; a universe full of only immortals is one that can swiftly become stagnant and resistant to new ideas. Those who delve into death's mysteries find these hidden depths to its grim embrace. It's extremely rare for whole societies to devote themselves to the way of death, but groups with such a dedication exist, and they often deliver mercy to those in need of release, repose to the undead, and solace to those left behind in death's wake. Even so, they are sometimes feared as killers or bringers of death by others who don't understand their ways.

Earth

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.svEkmRYZaxWiuhpE]{Earth}

Come what may, the earth endures. But that's not as simple as it sounds. The earth endures not merely by staying firm as a boulder, but by taking many forms, from unyielding mountains to shifting sands. Those who associate themselves with earth tend to be stubborn, though that doesn't mean they refuse to change their mind when presented with a new viewpoint. Instead, they hold their ground when necessary and shift it strategically. By always keeping one foot on metaphorical solid ground, they can ensure that they remain stable while forging a path ahead. None can deny the might and staying power of earth, but one of its limitations is a tendency to focus on either the extreme long term or short term, less so than the middle ground. This is only natural: when necessary, earth concentrates power close at hand and takes action right away, and otherwise, it can endure for eons. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of earth, they value that tenacity and explore the deeper nuances of earth's teachings, as seen among members of the kragrak ancestry.

Electricity

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.9KtNlRXeuxZSoVaI]{Electricity}

Electricity builds up charge and potential, releasing a powerful discharge of crackling lightning when the potential difference becomes great enough. Like the lightning, adherents who emulate the element of electricity understand that their energy and effort have different effects in a constant flowing current than they do if stored up and discharged strategically at the right time and place. Oftentimes, the resistance of opposing forces means that a steady, repetitive stream of effort in life can lead to a grind and burnout, while charging oneself up to capacity and then releasing a burst of creativity can allow an electricity enthusiast to accomplish challenges that seemed impossible. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of electricity, they often experience punctuated equilibria, where they plan and lay the groundwork, "building up charge," to prepare for explosive growth or innovation.

Fire

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.jddzxHC63KS1mXQ2]{Fire}

Fire's tendency to burn and grow, possibly ramping up out of control to devour everything around it, leads many in the world to see fire as mainly a destructive force. But to followers of the Eld, fire represents a change of state that can be either a bane or a boon. After all, periodic smaller wildfires can prevent a larger and more catastrophic blaze, and fire is necessary for many of civilization's innovations, from light sources to metallurgy to cooking and beyond. Nonetheless, fire is hungry and everspreading, until it chokes itself out by consuming all of the fuel available. To some Eld philosophers, this makes fire similar to a colony of industrious but infinitely voracious ants. Those who follow in fire's footsteps are equally unrestrained and equally ready to become or create something new, even if doing so forever changes them or the world around them in irreversible ways. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of fire, they can produce great works marvelous enough to be admired for millennia to come… but often these aren't without a sacrifice, as these groups or societies are inclined to seek artistic expression or scientific progress no matter the cost.

Force

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.xukaQBtERLxrw3q2]{Force}

The element of force weaves a web filled with strands of aether, congealed force, moving others around as it pleases like a puppet master. When those who don't understand the Eld hear the word "force," however, they often think of phrases like "brute force" and expect the force element to be unsubtle and fierce, causing great collateral damage. It's true that force is capable of great exertions and powerful assaults, but because of its flexible nature, force is often much subtler in the way it affects the world around it. Those Eld adherents who seek to emulate force are thus often more careful and deliberate about the way they interact with others, occasionally acting more detached, like chess masters moving pieces on a board. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of force, they pay attention to the connections and threads between them, learning how each member of the community exercises a push and pull of force on each other through their daily routine. This can lead force followers to act as an organic whole in a way that seems mystifying or almost psychic to outsiders, as one community member acts in advance almost as if to anticipate another member's needs. But the truth is that this is a bond forged not of telepathic communication but purely through a deep understanding of the forces within the community and their equal but opposite reactions.

Ice

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.KE6NkBCVI3YhyS89]{Ice}

Ice is the creeping cold that chills and saps away mobility until eventually all is quiet and still. The peace and stasis that ice brings can assist with quiet contemplation when wild burning ideas and excesses of kinetic energy would simply serve as a distraction. Nonetheless, for most creatures, too much cold is equally deadly to a fire's blaze. Those who seek to emulate the ice element always pause before making a decision and act slowly and decisively, rather than rashly. They appreciate a more deliberate approach, rather than rushing in spells and powers blazing. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of ice, they are often communities that remain the same in the face of tumultuous change, advancing slowly until they feel almost out of time with the world around them. These communities tend to be isolated, in order to keep themselves frozen despite the frenetic flow of history. Despite the use of "cold-blooded" and "ice-hearted" to mean emotionless or cruel, communities venerating ice often have deep emotional bonds because of their love of taking things slowly. That said, few have the kind of sense of humor that would appreciate the inevitable puns others make about their communities being isolated.

Life

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.NhIm0aUm5gCfNNVK]{Life}

Life is creation, a surge of vital energy, and the birth of new ideas, creatures, and things. Many see life as an inherently good thing, but it is neither good nor evil. What is born anew could be wonderful, but it could just as easily be awful. Deadly parasites and plagues are living things, and the life element nurtures their birth just as easily as it does a mortal infant. There is no bias in the cycle of life. When a cancerous growth or foul virus is just as deserving of life as any other living creature, it becomes clear that life is impassive and impartial, not a benefactor or force of good. Those who seek to follow in life's footsteps seek to create and nurture rather than end or destroy. Normally that makes them excellent friends or neighbors, but it can lead to problems when they insist on extending something past its normal limits or creating something that should have never been. Similarly, when entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of life, they often give birth to a new age or movement (or a renaissance of nascent or dormant ideas), but depending on what they birth, this isn't always a good thing for the societies and world around them.

Light

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.49UCcHG14STFKO2x]{Light}

Light reveals that which is hidden, allowing creatures to glimpse the truth it illuminates… but light is made up of various colors, each of which tints that truth in various ways. To followers of the Eld, there is no single objective truth, and so it's inevitable that the light element's revelations contain within them a factor of curation: whosoever discloses a given truth is the one who can decide the shape and details of the truth. Even when people can agree on a shared belief or truth, sometimes the harsh light of truth can be painful, meaning light isn't always a benevolent force. Those who seek to emulate light understand the inherent power that control of the truth gives a person, and few who follow the Eld seek to abuse that power to manipulate others. Even so, the ability to shape attention and spin narratives grants light-wielding demagogues great power. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of light, they are committing themselves to a radical honesty that can be painful, sometimes excruciatingly so. But they believe that this complete transparency will lead to better communication and a shared understanding and purpose.

Metal

Metal is an element of change and metamorphosis, but one with a purpose, not simply for change's sake. Shaping and reshaping. This concept follows the transition of metal, which is smelted from ore into a refined metal product and then forged into countless metal tools. Metal contains an endless potential to become, and as an element, its flexibility and utility are paramount. Eld adherents who revere the metal element in particular seek to shape the world around them, as well as to reforge themselves into something better. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of metal, they adapt and change on a regular basis, typically in an orderly fashion, in order to adapt to the world around them. This often means they have a form of government that rotates every certain number of years and in response to external stimuli, such as a representative democracy.

Mind

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.e4i0zJeUv8arEbVj]{Mind}

Mind is an element of infinite possibilities, since anything that can possibly be conceived or imagined can become a mental thoughtform or idea. Knowledge and logic derive from mind, and an assault using the mind element can sap a creature's cognitive processes and will. Mind embodies its own inner world of concept and thought. Those who follow the Eld and focus themselves on the mind element tend to be cerebral, meditating on deep fundamental questions and learning everything they can on a variety of topics. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of mind, they sometimes attempt to elevate thoughtfulness and mindfulness above irrational actions born out of gut responses and feelings. Even so, they ultimately learn that all of these things are tied to mind in their own way, and that a balance between the different forms of thought leads to a healthy mindscape and better mental health.

Music

Music is one of the most elusive of the twenty elements to define, and perhaps the most surprising element to those who don't follow or understand the Eld. Some who are ignorant wonder if music isn't simply a product of civilization. And yet, birds, bats, and many other animals sing, and even inanimate processes in the natural world can produce music in the right circumstances. In truth, music is a powerful element hidden within the harmonics of sound, one that a person can solve by mathematics, but also one that a force of nature can stumble upon by chance. The power of music to affect creatures on a deeper level than the sonic vibrations would produce on their own is a power in combination, a gestalt where the whole is far greater than the sum of its parts. A single note has meaning, but far less meaning and effect than when they're combined together. In this way, scholars believe that the music element was the first language and progenitor of all languages, the key that allows for effects like truespeech allowing beings to communicate universally. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of music, they seek to work together harmoniously, just as the notes and chords build together into a melody, and the instruments combine into a symphony. If everyone does their own different part, they can achieve far more than if they tried to do everything alone.

Poison

Whether or not a substance is toxic is subjective to an individual kind of creature, sometimes even among different creatures of the same species. But the element of poison is a universal incarnation of the concept of toxicity and its effects. Poison is an intractable element that disrupts the natural order of creatures' bodies until they no longer function. The poison element damages and weakens, and for nonliving things, it decays and dissolves them like acid. In this way, it acts as a universal poison; one that's toxic to the entire universe. Those who seek to emulate poison are disruptors and saboteurs who make their mark over time by undermining a more powerful opposition until it inevitably falls. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of poison, they often act as secret societies, infiltrating a larger settlement and eroding its norms and functions until it collapses, or changes in a different way.

Space

Space is the empty vastness beyond all that is. All that exists, exists within space. It's no mistake that the word space can mean both an emptiness and a physical location. Gaps provide distance, and in turn define a location. And thus, control over space allows someone to bend those rules, twisting space to bring two distant places together. Someone strongly attuned to the philosophy of the space element understands that emptying oneself of all that is unneeded can allow a person to center themselves and learn their place in the greater cosmos. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of space, other cultures tend to have trouble understanding that society's concept of place. Rather than lay claim to a nation or settlement with set boundaries, they see their polity as existing in whatever space holds it. However, that doesn't mean they ignore the world around them either. At the same time, they have an extremely fine-tuned sense of direction and distance, and they often create detailed maps of their surroundings.

Spirit

Spirit is the metaphysical building block of the soul, as well as the spiritual planes of existence that make up the afterlife for many mortal beings. Creatures who lose their physical body and continue to exist as disembodied souls are made of this element, and even celestials and fiends are built out of embodied spirit, also called quintessence, rather than the matter of the Material Plane. Spirit represents a deeper metaphysical layer of reality, which can lead to powerful attachments, in the same way that ghosts haunt a particular person, object, or place. A physical hunger is part of the life element, an intellectual appreciation is mind, but a deeper spiritual love is connected to the element of spirit, and so too can a hatred or grudge be so deep-rooted that it inscribes itself on a creature's very soul. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of spirit, they learn how to look past the surface level of the body in order to understand the spirit within, engaging deeply in spiritual practices and considering a person's health to be a measure of not only their physical wellbeing but also their spiritual wellness.

Time

Time is a dimension orthogonal to space, a perception of the jumbled chaos of reality as existing on a line that flows from past to future through the present, where events occur in order, causing other events and ever-rippling outward. Some in the world take time for granted, but philosophers of the Eld see these deeper truths. Time is a fundamental element of reality, but it's also a construct that mortals use to understand the entirety of existence and to wrest it all into a narrative or story they can more easily comprehend. Those who study how to manipulate the element of time learn how to adjust the way that they and others interact with time, dilating it, bending it, stuttering through it, and changing their reality via magical means, while those more interested in its teachings instead focus on centering themselves within the narrative flow, as well as understanding their timeline and the cause and effects of their actions. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of time, they begin to view the present as but one tiny infinitesimal point on the greater timeline, causing them to focus deeply on remembering and learning from the past while planning for and forging the future they wish to make.

Water

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.KBpZfx0zcl8Xb6CK]{Water}

Water is in constant motion, with the shifting tides and ebb and flow of the ocean and the roaring rush of the river. Even seemingly still water is part of the water cycle of evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. You can never step in the same river or ocean twice, as the water is ever-changing. Those Eld adherents who seek to emulate water learn to go with the flow, taking advantage of the unique opportunities of the changing situation rather than trying to keep things constantly the same. To submit to the flow is to lose control and normalcy, but it brings with it power and flexibility. A true master of water rides the tide both high and low, rather than try to control the tide and push it ever higher, leading to disaster. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of water, they prove flexible and opportunistic, able to handle the demands of a changing world, even during times of great upheaval, riding the tides of history to a brighter future. But as a result, they don't always have a single focused belief of their own and can be carried along by the enthusiasm or impetus of others.

Wood

@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.eldamon-features.Item.eE4dwFUMhlcxpetY]{Wood}

Wood is the element of wild growth and rampant verdure, representing the cycle of plant life from seed to towering oak. Wood encourages growth, but its nature isn't purely additive. Those who follow the teachings of the wood element know that too much unchecked growth can lead to problems where none of the plants can survive, and so sometimes the right thing to do is to harvest prudently in pursuit of greater expansion. So too do some plants lure other creatures to eat from them, in order to spread their seeds or pollen far afield. When entire groups or societies devote themselves to the way of wood, they tend to be farming communities with a strong eye toward sustainability and living in tune with nature. They sow different kinds of seeds and harvest them carefully, replenishing the soil and working with nature rather than against it.

Chapter 2: Character Classes.name: 第2章：角色职业 Chapter 2: Character Classes

Eldamon Trainer Key Terms.name: Eldamon 训练师关键术语 Eldamon Trainer Key Terms
Eldamon Trainer Key Terms.text:
你会在许多艾达兽训练师职业特性中看到以下术语。

相位：艾达兽以略微脱离现实的相位存在；而对艾达兽训练师而言，没有什么比学习如何与自己的艾达兽共享相位更为根本。为了让艾达兽能对除另一只脱离相位的艾达兽之外的任何目标使用其元素力量（或反过来受到影响），它们需要通过“相位艾达兽”动作切换进入物质世界的现实；这会使艾达兽训练师以等量但相反的反应脱离现实。多种专长与能力取决于艾达兽与训练师所处的相位。

力量：力量，也称为元素力量，是一种特殊的魔法能力类型，与法术不同。艾达兽与元素化身使用力量与其敌人战斗。一旦艾达兽或元素化身使用了一项力量，通常在使用一项刷新力量之前，他们无法再次使用该力量，不过该限制不适用于刷新力量本身。力量的攻击与DC使用彼此独立的熟练度，这些熟练度不同于法术、职业DC、武器或徒手的熟练度。若某项能力通常使用“你的职业DC或法术DC中较高者”，你总是可以在你的力量DC高于你的职业DC或法术DC时，以力量DC替代之。你的力量DC与力量攻击调整值基于你的关键属性。

刷新：带有刷新特征的力量会重新校准局部的元素能量，使艾达兽或元素化身能够再次使用其力量。所有刷新力量都包含一次“刷新”行动的使用。艾达兽与元素化身可以在不需要再次刷新（Refresh）的情况下任意次数使用刷新力量；这通常只在某个刷新力量被干扰时才会产生影响。

涌动：当艾达兽或元素化身无法使用某项力量时，他们有时会使用一种较弱的效果，称为元素涌动。有关元素涌动的更多细节见@UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.QsiJY2HP4WuA7Ntt.JournalEntryPage.eRfWb2pyr5IQMdrf#elemental-surge]{此处}。

终极：带有终极特征的力量极其强大，其运作方式不同于其他力量。终极力量会显著改变其周围的元素能量，并从使用它们的元素化身（或使用它们的艾达兽所属的艾达兽训练师）抽取大量能量。因此，在使用一次终极力量之后，该元素化身，或该元素训练师的艾达兽，在1小时内无法再次使用终极力量，因为这些能量需要时间凝聚并重塑。

You'll see the following terms in many eldamon trainer class features.

Phase: Eldamon exist slightly out of phase with reality, and for an eldamon trainer, nothing is more fundamental than learning how to share their own phase with their eldamon. In order for an eldamon to use their elemental powers against anything but another out of phase eldamon (or be affected in turn), they need to shift into the physical world's reality with the Phase Eldamon action, which shifts the eldamon trainer out of reality in an equal but opposite reaction. Various feats and abilities depend on the phase of the eldamon and trainer.

Power: Powers, also known as elemental powers, are a special type of magical ability, different from spells. Eldamon and elemental avatars use powers to battle their foes. Once an eldamon or elemental avatar uses a power, they normally can't use that power again until they use a refresh power, though that limit doesn't apply to refresh powers themselves. Powers have separate proficiencies for attacks and DCs, which aren't the same as spells, class DCs, or weapon or unarmed proficiencies. If an ability normally uses the "higher of your class DC or spell DC," you can always substitute your power DC if it's higher than either your class DC or spell DC. Your power DC and power attack modifier are based on your key attribute.

Refresh: A power with the refresh trait realigns the local elemental energies, allowing an eldamon or elemental avatar to use their powers again. All refresh powers include a use of the Refresh activity. Eldamon and elemental avatars can use refresh powers any number of times without needing to Refresh again; this normally only matters if a refresh power is disrupted.

Surge: When an eldamon or elemental avatar can't use a power, they sometimes use a weaker effect called an elemental surge. There are more details on elemental surges @UUID[Compendium.battlezoo-eldamon-pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.QsiJY2HP4WuA7Ntt.JournalEntryPage.eRfWb2pyr5IQMdrf#elemental-surge]{here}.

Ultimate: A power with the ultimate trait is extremely powerful and doesn't work like other powers. Ultimate powers significantly alter the elemental energies around them and take significant energy from the elemental avatar who uses them, or the eldamon trainer of the eldamon who uses them. As such, after using an ultimate power, that elemental avatar, or that elemental trainer's eldamon, can't use an ultimate power again for 1 hour, as the energies take time to coalesce and reshape.

Eldamon Trainer.name: 艾达魔训练师 Eldamon Trainer
Eldamon Trainer.text:
While many folk across the land might befriend an eldamon or two, you’ve always had a deeper connection to these mystical elemental beings, allowing you to bond with them on a deeper level. You can train them to phase into the material world by trading your own phase with theirs, and through growing your bond and understanding together, you can help them evolve a more powerful form. Whether fighting for your life or engaging in the mock battles eldamon enjoy, with your eldamon friends at your side, you’re going to become the best you can and prove yourself to the world!

Key Attribute

Charisma or other

At 1st level, your class gives you an attribute boost to Charisma or an option from your calling.

Hit Points

8 plus your Constitution Modifier

You increase your maximum number of HP by this number at 1st level and every level thereafter.

During Combat Encounters…

You phase yourself out and your eldamon in, choosing the perfect eldamon to handle the specifics of the battle and swapping or refreshing them as necessary to adapt to the changing situation.

During Social Encounters…

You use the same techniques you’ve perfected in befriending and training eldamon to make friends and influence people, taking charge when necessary to help solve problems and deal with the situation at hand.

While Exploring…

You keep an eye out for any unusual elemental effects, as well as for situations where your eldamon could help out, phasing in the appropriate eldamon to assist when necessary. When you’re using your eldamon extensively over a long distance, this involves shifting phase back and forth with your eldamon so you can catch up.

In Downtime…

You spend time with each of your eldamon, deepening your bonds of friendship, training them in new techniques, and helping them grow.

You Might…

  • Stop in the middle of things every so often to chat with your eldamon friends.
  • Always have a story and an eldamon for any situation.
  • Occasionally feel distant and out of place in the mortal world, as your essence recalls the time you've spent out of phase in place of your trained eldamon.

Others Probably…

  • Marvel at the many trained eldamon you can call upon to assist you.
  • Wonder if you care more about your eldamon friends than you do about your other friends and family.
  • Confuse you for a summoner, witch, druid, or other spellcaster focused on a companion or eidolon.

Initial Proficiencies

At 1st level, you gain the listed proficiency ranks in the following statistics. You are untrained in anything not listed unless you gain a better proficiency rank in some other way

Perception

Trained in Perception

Saving Throws

Trained in Fortitude

Trained in Reflex

Expert in Will

Skills

Trained in Diplomacy

Trained in a number of additional skills equal to 3 plus your Intelligence modifier

Attacks

Trained in simple weapons

Trained in unarmed attacks

Defenses

Trained in light armor

Trained in unarmored defense

Class DC

Trained in eldamon trainer class DC

Class Features

You gain these abilities as an eldamon trainer. Abilities gained at higher levels list the level at which you gain them next to the features' names.

Your LevelClass Features
1Ancestry and background, initial proficiencies, calling, eldamon powers, eldamon trainer feat, elemental assistance, elemental effusion, trained eldamon
2Eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
3Eldamon powers, fortitude expertise, general feat, skill increase
4Eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
5Advanced training, ancestry feat, attribute boosts eldamon powers, skill increase
6Eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
7Eldamon powers, general feat, reflex expertise, skill increase
8Eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
9Ancestry feat, eldamon expert, eldamon powers, skill increase, trainer’s heart
10Attribute boosts, eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
11Eldamon evolution, eldamon powers, general feat, skill increase, weapon expertise
12Eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
13Alertness, ancestry feat, eldamon powers, elemental defense, melded phase, skill increase, weapon specialization
14Eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
15Apex training, attribute boosts, eldamon powers, general feat, skill increase
16Eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
17Ancestry feat, eldamon master, eldamon powers, greater trainer’s heart, skill increase
18Eldamon trainer feat, skill feat
19General feat, skill increase, ultimate powers
20Attribute boosts, eldamon trainer feat, skill feat

Ancestry and Background

In addition to what you get from your class at 1st level, you have the benefits of your selected ancestry and background.

Initial Proficiencies

At 1st level, you gain a number of proficiencies that represent your basic training. These proficiencies are noted at the start of this class. You have access to the languages spoken by your starting eldamon, in case you want to choose them as starting languages; if you've already chosen your starting languages, you can go back and adjust them if you wish.

Trained Eldamon

You have a deeper metaphysical link to eldamon than others, allowing you to connect with them on a deeper level than most. This allows you to train eldamon and even to swap your personal phase with them, phasing out of the material world so your eldamon can phase in. You begin at 1st level with three eldamon, which can be either common eldamon of your choice or other eldamon to which you have access. You can befriend additional eldamon as normal (see page 28 for more details on befriending eldamon). Each day during your daily preparations, choose a number of eldamon equal to 3 or your key attribute modifier, whichever is less. You spend extra time with those eldamon, preparing to swap your phase with those eldamon throughout the day. Your eldamon gain levels alongside you as you train them, increasing to match your level whenever you level up. It's unlikely to befriend an eldamon of higher level than you, and even if you do, you can't share your phase with them.

Due to your metaphysical connection to your eldamon when you've swapped your personal phase with them, you share multiple attack penalties and actions (though you normally can't act), and you can use your Hero Points on their behalf.

Eldamon aren't the same as mortal creatures, and they don't die, even when it seems like they would be killed. An eldamon that reaches 0 Hit Points doesn't become unconscious or dying; they just phase out if they were phased in and become unresponsive. An eldamon that takes any amount of damage recovers all of their Hit Points after being phased out after an encounter, unless they were reduced to 0 Hit Points. If they were reduced to 0 Hit Points by an effect other than a death effect, they recover all their Hit Points after being phased out after the encounter for at least 1 minute. If reduced to 0 Hit Points by a death effect, an eldamon still doesn't die, but it does take longer for them to collect their energy. In that case, they remain unresponsive and out of phase for 1 hour.

You gain the Phase Eldamon activity and your eldamon gain the Tug Conduit action.

Lost and Altered Actions

Normally you can't act while you've shifted phases with your eldamon, but if you have a way to do so, you share your actions with your eldamon. If your eldamon becomes slowed, stunned, quickened, or otherwise affected by something that changes the actions you gain at the start of each turn, it affects your shared actions, but if you have such a condition, it ordinarily doesn't affect your shared actions (though if you're prevented from acting entirely, you can't use any special actions that work while you're phased out). The GM resolves any situation that's unclear.

You, as the player, control both your eldamon trainer and your eldamon, and the two are generally considered to cooperate unless you decide for them not to. Once you've shared your phase, however, the eldamon is in control until it relinquishes that phase. If there's ever a conflict of who should act, your eldamon wins out over you. This means that if the eldamon comes under an effect that would take their actions out of your control, such as the confused or controlled condition, your eldamon uses all the shared actions, preventing you from acting, and they can prevent you from forcibly phasing them out via Sudden Switch, Emergency Phase, and similar effects. Your eldamon can also prevent you from acting for your own good if you're confused or controlled while phased out, but this is much less likely to occur

What Is Phase?

Phase is a state of being, rather than another plane of existence. A being that is phased out is still visible to beings that are phased in, and can perceive the mortal world, albeit through a filter. The metaphysical underpinnings of the world still bind phased out creatures. They don't fall through the ground, they move along with a planet's frame of reference (or a particularly large vehicle such as a ship) rather than being pinned to a particular point in space, they can't move through walls and cross other boundaries, and so on. Beings who aren't used to being out of phase with the mortal world, such as mortal ancestries, have severe difficulty acting when they aren't in phase.

Calling

Every eldamon trainer has a calling. A calling corresponds to the reason the eldamon trainer travels the land befriending eldamon, but it’s more than just a reason. Those without the incredible will and absolute certainty to see their calling through rarely manifest a connection to eldamon strong enough to allow them to share their own personal phase with an eldamon. The following are the eldamon trainer callings presented in this book.

Eldamon Battler

Victory in battle is the most important thing to you, and to that end, you want to recruit the strongest possible team of eldamon who are willing to help you fight your way to the top. Everything else is a means to that end for you, but that doesn't mean you don't care just as deeply for your eldamon as trainers of other callings. You train with them every day, working to help them actualize their goals and reach their hidden potential. You can't be the very best if you try to do everything on your own—you can only achieve victory together!

All of your eldamon gain the Elemental Shield action and increase their maximum Hit Points by an amount equal to your level (minimum 2). When your eldamon use the Elemental Shield action, it gains their element's associated trait or traits.

Eldamon Researcher

Eldamon are endlessly fascinating creatures, distinct from mortals, constructs, undead, and even other extraplanar creatures. They embody a cross-section of reality that mortal ancestries can rarely see or access, at least under normal conditions, and thus they represent a unique opportunity to understand the metaphysical underpinnings of the cosmos! For this and many other reasons, countless researchers have become obsessed with studying every aspect of eldamon, and you are among their esteemed number.

You can choose Intelligence as your key attribute. You're trained in Nature. You can use your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Wisdom modifier, to roll Nature checks to Recall Knowledge. You can always roll Nature for initiative against eldamon and elementals, using your Intelligence modifier instead of your Wisdom modifier if you prefer, and you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Nature checks to Recall Knowledge about eldamon and elementals and on initiative rolls against eldamon and elementals. Furthermore, your deep knowledge about eldamon and elementals grants you a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks and saving throws against eldamon and elementals. Finally, you gain the I Know This! Action.

Eldamon Whisperer

You feel a deeper connection to eldamon than anyone else you've met. Whether you find them adorable, majestic, or mysterious, they've always made a huge impression on you, and something inside you told you that your destiny was entwined with eldamon. For their part, eldamon have always reacted well to you, even compared to other eldamon trainers.

You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Diplomacy checks to Make an Impression on an eldamon, Make a Request of an eldamon, or use your elemental assistance class feature. You also gain the +2 circumstance bonus to all other skill checks made to build your relationship with your eldamon or to directly interact with your eldamon to reach an evolution threshold, though this doesn't apply to any checks you make during your adventures to help reach an evolution threshold; for example, if you needed to tell your eldamon a ghost story, the bonus would apply to the check you make to tell the ghost story, but if you needed to find valuable treasure or defeat a powerful enemy in battle, the circumstance bonus wouldn't apply to your checks to do so.

Additionally, whenever you meet an eldamon in a non-hostile environment, including as a typical meeting after defeating a creature it was inhabiting or finding an item it was inhabiting, you can immediately attempt a Diplomacy check to Make an Impression on that eldamon rather than needing to converse for 1 minute. You take a –5 penalty to the check. If you fail, you can engage in 1 minute of conversation and attempt a new check at the end of that time rather than accepting the failure or critical failure result. If you have the Glad-Hand skill feat, you don't take the penalty on your check if the target is an eldamon.

Finally, you gain the You Can Do It! Action.

Eldamon Trainer Feats

At 1st level and every even-numbered level, you gain an eldamon trainer class feat.

Eldamon Powers

Your eldamon are capable of incredible powers based on their element. Powers, also known as elemental powers, are a special type of magical ability, different from spells. When phased into reality, eldamon can use their powers to battle your foes. Once an eldamon uses a power, they normally can't use that power again until they use a refresh power (though that limit doesn't apply to refresh powers themselves), or until they phase back out, whichever comes first. Powers have separate proficiencies for attacks and DCs, which aren't the same as spells, class DCs, or weapon or unarmed proficiencies. Your eldamon share several types of item bonus with you if they come from invested items (see page 156 for more information on eldamon and items). An eldamon's power DC and power attack modifiers are based on their key attribute, Wisdom.

When you first befriend an eldamon, they typically know the powers listed for that eldamon at that level, though you might find an eldamon with different powers.

At 3rd level, 5th level, 7th level, 9th level, 11th level, 13th level, 15th level, and 17th level, your eldamon can replace one of their elemental powers with a new elemental power, which must be of their level or lower. It's simplest and saves a lot of time if you befriend numerous eldamon to use the suggested progression listed for that eldamon, but since you're training them, you can work together with them to learn whichever power you wish. At 19th level, they instead learn an ultimate power (see the ultimate power class feature for more details).

Too Many Eldamon!

There are a huge number of eldamon, elements, and elemental powers. Fortunately, the rules have you covered in case you want to keep it simple. For a simpler experience, don't give access to other eldamon, limiting the starting eldamon to 13 options, and stick with the default powers gained when leveling up. To simplify even further and avoid needing to read 13 elements' worth of powers to decide which common eldamon to choose, use one of these sets of three starting eldamon. Sets that include ankhrub, a life eldamon, tend to be more flexible to the needs of combat, as they allow for a very different playstyle from other elements.

Balanced: Ankhrub, Squirrelash, Tairopoof

Classic: Squirrelash, Boteau, Lotusnek

Energetic: Chillot, Tawhirtle, Potfox

Esoteric: Ankhrub, Morpup, Meese

Simple: Squirrelash, Kitopaz, Morpup

Elemental Assistance

During exploration, you phase in eldamon to use their elemental powers in order to assist your allies. You can Aid one of your allies’ skill checks during exploration using Diplomacy to determine the result of your Aid as you encourage your eldamon to assist with their elemental powers. To do so, describe how the eldamon’s elemental abilities assist your ally’s check.

Elemental Effusion

Elemental energy tends to pool and gather around you over time as it remains unused, waiting to spill out when first released. Just before you phase in your first eldamon during an encounter, as you alter your phase, elemental energy that matches the eldamon you intend to phase in spills out from you whether you want it to or not. You can direct that energy to manifest into a small elemental assault at a foe to deal minor damage and test the foe's resistances. Just before you phase in your first eldamon during a battle, choose a foe within 60 feet and choose an associated damage type of the eldamon you intend to phase in. The foe must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC with the following effects. You can spend a single action on your Phase Eldamon to switch the eldamon you're phasing in to a different eldamon based on the information you learned from the effusion.

Skill Feats Level 2

At 2nd level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a skill feat. You must be trained or better in the corresponding skill to select a skill feat.

Fortitude Expertise Level 3

Your physique is incredibly hardy. Your proficiency rank for Fortitude saves increases to expert.

General Feats Level 3

At 3rd level and every 4 levels thereafter, you gain a general feat.

Skill Increases Level 3

At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a skill increase. You can use this increase to either become trained in one skill you're untrained in, or become an expert in one skill in which you're already trained.

At 7th level, you can use skill increases to become a master in a skill in which you're already an expert, and at 15th level, you can use them to become legendary in a skill in which you're already a master.

Attribute Boosts Level 5

At 5th level and every 5 levels thereafter, you get four free boosts to different attribute modifiers. If an attribute modifier is already +4 or higher, it takes two boosts to increase it; you get a partial boost, and must boost that attribute again at a later level to increase it by 1.

Ancestry Feats Level 5

In addition to the ancestry feat you started with, you gain an ancestry feat at 5th level and every 4 levels thereafter.

Advanced Training Level 5
